revision.comment: Auto generating page. date placed far in the past so it is attributed to Immanuelle and FireflyBot sends them messages if it is ever imported to Wikipedia.

revision.comment: [[WP:AES|←]]新しいページ: '[[ファイル:Sangu-jinja Shinonsen03s5s4592.jpg|thumb|300px|山宮神社]] '''山宮神社'''（さんぐうじんじゃ）は[[兵庫県]][[新温泉町]]七...'
revision.text:
[[ファイル:Sangu-jinja Shinonsen03s5s4592.jpg|thumb|300px|山宮神社]] '''山宮神社'''（さんぐうじんじゃ）は[[兵庫県]][[新温泉町]]七釜字木谷1409に鎮座する[[神社]]。旧[[社格]]は[[村社]]。主祭神は[[大山祇命]]、副祭神は[[イザナギ]]、[[イザナミ]]、[[誉田別尊]]、[[スサノオノミコト]]。
==概要== [[七釜温泉]]源泉近くの小高い山林の上に位置する。創建年代は不明。近世以前は'''山神権現'''と称されていた祖神で、明治初年に現在の名称に改められた。[[明治6年]]3月、村社列格と同時に[[八幡神]]と胞衣神を合祀した。
[[室町時代]]の[[文安]]元年に再建された社殿は、[[昭和19年]]に台風で倒壊する。現在の社殿は戦後建立されたものである。
==祭事・行事== *例祭日 9月29日
==文化財== *七釜[[麒麟獅子]]舞 - 町指定民俗文化財（1986年3月31日指定） *山宮神社社叢 - 町指定文化財天然記念物（1986年3月31日指定） 、スダジイやヒノキの古木、ヤブツバキ・サカキなどが繁茂する鎮守の森
==交通アクセス== *[[西日本旅客鉄道|JR西日本]][[山陰本線]]：[[浜坂駅]]から[[全但バス]][[湯村温泉 (兵庫県)|湯村温泉]]行で、バス停「七釜温泉口」下車 徒歩5分 *大阪・神戸から全但バス[[夢千代号]]で「湯村温泉」下車、浜坂行バスに乗り換えて「七釜温泉口」下車 徒歩5分
==周辺== *[[浜坂温泉郷]] **[[浜坂温泉]] - バス9分 *[[湯村温泉 (兵庫県)|湯村温泉]] - バス15分 *[[浜坂県民サンビーチ]] *[[但馬御火浦]]
==外部リンク== {{Commonscat|Sangu-jinja}}
{{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:しちかまおんせん}}
[[Category:新温泉町]] [[Category:兵庫県の神社]] [[Category:村社]]

[[ファイル:Sangu-jinja Shinonsen03s5s4592.jpg|thumb|300px|山宮神社]] '''山宮神社'''（さんぐうじんじゃ）は[[兵庫県]][[新温泉町]]七釜字木谷1409に鎮座する[[神社]]。旧[[社格]]は[[村社]]。主祭神は[[大山祇命]]、副祭神は[[イザナギ]]、[[イザナミ]]、[[誉田別尊]]、[[スサノオノミコト]]。
==概要== [[七釜温泉]]源泉近くの小高い山林の上に位置する。創建年代は不明。近世以前は'''山神権現'''と称されていた祖神で、[[明治元年]]に現在の名称に改められた。[[明治6年]]3月、村社列格と同時に[[八幡神]]と胞衣神を合祀した。
[[室町時代]]の[[文安]]元年に再建された社殿は、[[昭和19年]]に台風で倒壊する。現在の社殿は戦後建立されたものである。
==祭事・行事== *例祭日 9月29日
==文化財== *七釜[[麒麟獅子]]舞 - 町指定民俗文化財（1986年3月31日指定） *山宮神社社叢 - 町指定文化財天然記念物（1986年3月31日指定） 、スダジイやヒノキの古木、ヤブツバキ・サカキなどが繁茂する鎮守の森
==交通アクセス== *[[西日本旅客鉄道|JR西日本]][[山陰本線]]：[[浜坂駅]]から[[全但バス]][[湯村温泉 (兵庫県)|湯村温泉]]行で、バス停「七釜温泉口」下車 徒歩5分 *大阪・神戸から全但バス[[夢千代号]]で「湯村温泉」下車、浜坂行バスに乗り換えて「七釜温泉口」下車 徒歩5分
==周辺== *[[浜坂温泉郷]] **[[浜坂温泉]] - バス9分 *[[湯村温泉 (兵庫県)|湯村温泉]] - バス15分 *[[浜坂県民サンビーチ]] *[[但馬御火浦]]
==外部リンク== {{Commonscat|Sangu-jinja}}
{{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:しちかまおんせん}}

{{神社 |名称 = 山宮神社 |画像 = [[ファイル:Sangu-jinja Shinonsen03s5s4592.jpg|300px]] |所在地 = 兵庫県美方郡新温泉町七釜字木谷1409 |位置 = |祭神 = 大山祇命 |社格 = |創建 = 不明 |本殿 = |別名 = |例祭 = 9月29日 |神事 = |}} '''山宮神社'''（さんぐうじんじゃ）は[[兵庫県]][[美方郡]][[新温泉町]]七釜字木谷1409に鎮座する[[神社]]。旧[[社格]]は[[村社]]。主祭神は[[大山祇命]]、副祭神は[[イザナギ]]、[[イザナミ]]、[[誉田別尊]]、[[スサノオノミコト]]。

revision.text:
{{神社 |名称 = 山宮神社 |画像 = [[ファイル:Sangu-jinja Shinonsen03s5s4592.jpg|300px]] |所在地 = [[兵庫県]][[美方郡]][[新温泉町]]七釜1409 |位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|36|39.6|N|134|28|44.7|E|region:JP-28_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |祭神 = [[大山祇命]] |社格 = |創建 = 不明 |本殿 = |別名 = |例祭 = 9月29日 |神事 = ||地図={{Location map|Japan Hyogo|lat_deg=35|lat_min=36|lat_sec=39.6|lon_deg=134|lon_min=28|lon_sec=44.7|label=山宮神社|position=center|width=200|float=center}}}} '''山宮神社'''（さんぐうじんじゃ）は、[[兵庫県]][[美方郡]][[新温泉町]]七釜字木谷1409に鎮座する[[神社]]。旧[[社格]]は[[村社]]。主祭神は[[大山祇命]]、副祭神は[[イザナギ]]、[[イザナミ]]、[[誉田別尊]]、[[スサノオノミコト]]。
== 概要 == [[七釜温泉]]源泉近くの小高い山林の上に位置する。創建年代は不明。近世以前は'''山神権現'''と称されていた祖神で、[[1868年]]（[[明治]]元年）に現在の名称に改められた。[[1873年]]（明治6年）3月、村社列格と同時に[[八幡神]]と胞衣神を合祀した。
[[室町時代]]の[[1444年]]（[[文安]]元年）に再建された社殿は、[[1944年]]（[[昭和]]19年）に台風で倒壊する。現在の社殿は戦後建立されたものである。
==祭事・行事== *例祭日 9月29日
==文化財== *七釜[[麒麟獅子]]舞 - 町指定民俗文化財（1986年3月31日指定） *山宮神社社叢 - 町指定文化財天然記念物（1986年3月31日指定） 、スダジイやヒノキの古木、ヤブツバキ・サカキなどが繁茂する鎮守の森
==交通アクセス== *[[西日本旅客鉄道|JR西日本]][[山陰本線]]：[[浜坂駅]]から[[全但バス]][[湯村温泉 (兵庫県)|湯村温泉]]行で、バス停「七釜温泉口」下車 徒歩5分 *大阪・神戸から全但バス[[夢千代号]]で「湯村温泉」下車、浜坂行バスに乗り換えて「七釜温泉口」下車 徒歩5分
==周辺== *[[浜坂温泉郷]] **[[浜坂温泉]] - バス9分 *[[湯村温泉 (兵庫県)|湯村温泉]] - バス15分 *[[浜坂県民サンビーチ]] *[[但馬御火浦]]
==関連項目== *[[山宮神社]]
==外部リンク== {{Commonscat|Sangu-jinja}}{{神道 横}}{{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:さんくうしんしや}} [[Category:新温泉町]] [[Category:新温泉町の歴史]] [[Category:兵庫県の神社]] [[Category:兵庫県の旧村社]]

'''山宮神社'''（さんぐうじんじゃ、やまみやじんじゃ）は[[日本]]の[[神社]]。各地に存在する。
*山宮神社 - [[山梨県]][[笛吹市]]鎮座の浅間神社の元宮。⇒[[浅間神社 (笛吹市)]] *[[山宮神社 (新温泉町)]] - [[兵庫県]][[新温泉町]]鎮座の神社。 *[[山宮神社 (鹿屋市)]] - [[鹿児島県]][[鹿屋市]]串良細山田鎮座の神社。 *山宮神社 (志布志市志布志町安楽) - 鹿児島県[[志布志市]]志布志町安楽鎮座の神社。⇒[[安楽山宮神社]]。 *山宮神社 (志布志市志布志町田之浦) - 鹿児島県[[志布志市]]志布志町田之浦鎮座の神社。⇒[[田ノ浦山宮神社]]。
{{曖昧さ回避}}
{{DEFAULTSORT:さんくうしんしや}} [[Category:同名の神社]]

'''Sangu Shrine''' (山宮神社, also read as Yama-miya Shrine) refers to Shinto shrines found throughout Japan.
* Sangu Shrine - The original shrine (Moto-miya) of {{ill|Asama Shrine (Fuefuki)|en|浅間神社 (笛吹市)}} in {{ill|Fuefuki|en|笛吹市}}, {{ill|Yamanashi Prefecture|en|山梨県}}. * {{ill|Sangu Shrine (Shinonsen)|en|山宮神社 (新温泉町)}} - Located in {{ill|Shinonsen|en|新温泉町}}, {{ill|Hyogo Prefecture|en|兵庫県}}. * {{ill|Sangu Shrine (Kanoya)|en|山宮神社 (鹿屋市)}} - Located in {{ill|Kanoya|en|鹿屋市}}, {{ill|Kagoshima Prefecture|en|鹿児島県}}, in the Kushira Hosoyamada area. * Sangu Shrine (Shibushi, Anraku) - Located in {{ill|Shibushi|en|志布志市}}, Kagoshima Prefecture, in the Anraku area of {{ill|Shibushi Town (Anraku)|en|志布志町安楽}}. See {{ill|Anraku Sangu Shrine|en|安楽山宮神社}}. * Sangu Shrine (Shibushi, Tanoura) - Located in Shibushi City, Kagoshima Prefecture, in the Tanoura area of {{ill|Shibushi Town (Tanoura)|en|志布志町田之浦}}. See {{ill|Tanoura Sangu Shrine|en|田ノ浦山宮神社}}.
{{Disambiguation}}
[[Category:Shrines with identical names]] [[ja:山宮神社]]

revision.text:
'''Sangu Shrine''' (山宮神社, also read as Yama-miya Shrine) refers to Shinto shrines found throughout Japan.
* Sangu Shrine - The original shrine (Moto-miya) of {{ill|Ichinomiya Asama Shrine (Fuefuki)|ja|浅間神社 (笛吹市)}} in {{ill|Fuefuki|ja|笛吹市}}, {{ill|Yamanashi Prefecture|ja|山梨県}}. * {{ill|Sangu Shrine (Shinonsen)|ja|山宮神社 (新温泉町)}} - Located in {{ill|Shinonsen|ja|新温泉町}}, {{ill|Hyogo Prefecture|ja|兵庫県}}. * {{ill|Sangu Shrine (Kanoya)|ja|山宮神社 (鹿屋市)}} - Located in {{ill|Kanoya|ja|鹿屋市}}, {{ill|Kagoshima Prefecture|ja|鹿児島県}}, in the Kushira Hosoyamada area. * Sangu Shrine (Shibushi, Anraku) - Located in {{ill|Shibushi|ja|志布志市}}, Kagoshima Prefecture, in the Anraku area of {{ill|Shibushi Town (Anraku)|ja|志布志町安楽}}. See {{ill|Anraku Sangu Shrine|ja|安楽山宮神社}}. * Sangu Shrine (Shibushi, Tanoura) - Located in Shibushi City, Kagoshima Prefecture, in the Tanoura area of {{ill|Shibushi Town (Tanoura)|ja|志布志町田之浦}}. See {{ill|Tanoura Sangu Shrine|ja|田ノ浦山宮神社}}.
{{Disambiguation}}

| coordinates = {{coord|35|36|39.6|N|134|28|44.7|E|region:JP-28_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}
| deity = {{ill|Ōyamatsumi|ja|大山祇命|1=Ōyamatsumi|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamatsumi|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamatsumi|qq=|ja_comment=fixed jawiki redirect|3=オオヤマツミ}}

}}

'''Sangu Shrine''' (山宮神社, *Sangu Jinja*) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located at Shichikama, Shin'onsen, Mikata District, [[:en:Hyōgo Prefecture|Hyōgo Prefecture]]. It was formerly classified as a {{ill|Village shrine|ja|村社|1=Son-sha|qid=Q134917284}}{{efn|Former shrine rank|lt=Son-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Village shrine|qq=|ja_comment=fixed jawiki redirect|3=近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}. The main deity enshrined is {{ill|Ōyamatsumi|ja|大山祇命|1=Ōyamatsumi|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamatsumi|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamatsumi|qq=|ja_comment=fixed jawiki redirect|3=オオヤマツミ}}, with additional enshrinements of [[:en:Izanagi|Izanagi]], [[:en:Izanami|Izanami]], {{ill|Homuda Wake no Mikoto|ja|誉田別尊|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Homuda Wake no Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=fixed jawiki redirect|3=応神天皇|1=Ōjin|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q317997}}, and {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|スサノオノミコト|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=fixed jawiki redirect|3=スサノオ}}.

== Overview ==
The shrine is located atop a small forested hill near the source of the {{ill|Shichikama Onsen|ja|七釜温泉|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shichikama Onsen|qq=|1=Shichikama Onsen|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11353724}}. The exact founding date is unknown. Before the modern era, it was known as '''Sangami Gongen''' (山神権現). In [[:en:1868|1868]] (the first year of the [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]]), it was renamed to its current title. In [[:en:1873|1873]] (Meiji 6), it was designated a village shrine and enshrined [[:en:Hachiman|Hachiman]] and the local {{ill|Placenta deity|ja|胞衣神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Placenta deity|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.

The shrine's main hall was rebuilt in [[:en:1444|1444]] (Bun'an 1, [[:en:Muromachi period|Muromachi period]]), but was destroyed in [[:en:1944|1944]] (Shōwa 19) due to a typhoon. The current structure was rebuilt after World War II.

== Festivals and Events ==
* Annual Festival: September 29<ref>{{Cite web|title=Festival Calendar - Sangu Shrine|url=https://www.kirinnomachi-japan-heritage.jp/jp/reisai/1059/|access-date=2022-12-19|publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs}}</ref>

== Cultural Properties ==
* '''Shichikama {{ill|Kirin Shishi-mai|ja|麒麟獅子舞|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kirin Shishi-mai|qq=|ja_comment=fixed jawiki redirect|3=麒麟獅子|1=Qilin dance|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11677329}} Dance''' - Town-designated Intangible Folk Cultural Property (designated March 31, 1986)<ref>{{Cite web|title=Shichikama Kirin Shishi-mai|url=https://www.town.shinonsen.hyogo.jp/page/?mode=detail&page_id=8ab359199e3f3c495d46db6ceb3791ec|access-date=2022-12-19|publisher=Shin'onsen Town}}</ref>
* '''Sangu Shrine Sacred Forest''' (山宮神社社叢) - Town-designated Natural Monument (designated March 31, 1986), featuring ancient [[:en:Castanopsis sieboldii|Castanopsis]] and [[:en:Chamaecyparis obtusa|Hinoki cypress]] trees, along with thick growths of {{ill|Camellia japonica|ja|ヤブツバキ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Camellia japonica|qq=|ja_comment=fixed jawiki redirect|3=ツバキ|1=Camellia japonica|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q160121}} and [[:en:Cleyera japonica|Sakaki]].<ref>{{Cite web|title=Sacred Forest of Shichikama Sangu Shrine|url=https://www.town.shinonsen.hyogo.jp/page/?mode=detail&page_id=b500773414376fa6bd4970ec294860e2|access-date=2022-12-19|publisher=Shin'onsen Town}}</ref>

== Access ==
* [[:en:West Japan Railway Company|West Japan Railway Company]] (JR West) [[:en:San'in Main Line|San'in Main Line]]: From [[:en:Hamasaka Station|Hamasaka Station]], take the {{ill|Zentan Bus|ja|全但バス|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zentan Bus|qq=|1=Zentan Bus|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11389110}} bound for {{ill|Yumura Onsen (Hyōgo)|ja|湯村温泉|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yumura Onsen (Hyōgo)|qq=|1=Yumura Onsen (Hyōgo)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11563591}}, and get off at "Shichikama Onsen-guchi" bus stop. 5-minute walk.
* From Osaka or Kobe, take the Zentan Bus {{ill|Yumechiyo-go|ja|夢千代号|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yumechiyo-go|qq=|ja_comment=fixed jawiki redirect|3=全但バス|1=Zentan Bus|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11389110}} to "Yumura Onsen," transfer to the bus for Hamasaka, and get off at "Shichikama Onsen-guchi." 5-minute walk.

== Nearby Attractions ==
* {{ill|Hamasaka Onsenkyō|ja|浜坂温泉郷|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hamasaka Onsenkyō|qq=|1=Hamasaka Onsenkyō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11557619}}
** {{ill|Hamasaka Onsen|ja|浜坂温泉|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hamasaka Onsen|qq=|1=Hamasaka Onsen|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11557617}} (9-minute bus ride)
* {{ill|Yumura Onsen (Hyōgo)|ja|湯村温泉|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yumura Onsen (Hyōgo)|qq=|1=Yumura Onsen (Hyōgo)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11563591}} (15-minute bus ride)
* {{ill|Hamasaka Prefectural Sun Beach|ja|浜坂県民サンビーチ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hamasaka Prefectural Sun Beach|qq=|1=Hamasaka Kenmin Sun　Beach|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11557620}}
* {{ill|Tajima Mihonoura|ja|但馬御火浦|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tajima Mihonoura|qq=|1=Tajima Mihonoura|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11381630}}

'''Sangu Shrine''' (山宮神社, *Sangu Jinja*) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located at Shichikama, Shin'onsen, Mikata District, [[:en:Hyōgo Prefecture|Hyōgo Prefecture]]. It was formerly classified as a {{ill|Village shrine|ja|村社|1=Son-sha|WD=Q134917284}}{{efn|Former shrine rank|lt=Son-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Village shrine|ja_comment=fixed jawiki redirect|3=近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}. The main deity enshrined is {{ill|Ōyamatsumi|ja|大山祇命|1=Ōyamatsumi|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamatsumi|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamatsumi|ja_comment=fixed jawiki redirect|3=オオヤマツミ}}, with additional enshrinements of [[:en:Izanagi|Izanagi]], [[:en:Izanami|Izanami]], {{ill|Homuda Wake no Mikoto|ja|誉田別尊|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Homuda Wake no Mikoto|ja_comment=fixed jawiki redirect|3=応神天皇|1=Ōjin|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q317997}}, and {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|スサノオノミコト|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=fixed jawiki redirect|3=スサノオ}}.

== Overview ==
The shrine is located atop a small forested hill near the source of the {{ill|Shichikama Onsen|ja|七釜温泉|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shichikama Onsen|1=Shichikama Onsen|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11353724}}. The exact founding date is unknown. Before the modern era, it was known as '''Sangami Gongen''' (山神権現). In [[:en:1868|1868]] (the first year of the [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]]), it was renamed to its current title. In [[:en:1873|1873]] (Meiji 6), it was designated a village shrine and enshrined [[:en:Hachiman|Hachiman]] and the local {{ill|Placenta deity|ja|胞衣神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Placenta deity|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.

The shrine's main hall was rebuilt in [[:en:1444|1444]] (Bun'an 1, [[:en:Muromachi period|Muromachi period]]), but was destroyed in [[:en:1944|1944]] (Shōwa 19) due to a typhoon. The current structure was rebuilt after World War II.

== Festivals and Events ==
* Annual Festival: September 29<ref>{{Cite web|title=Festival Calendar - Sangu Shrine|url=https://www.kirinnomachi-japan-heritage.jp/jp/reisai/1059/|access-date=2022-12-19|publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs}}</ref>

== Cultural Properties ==
* '''Shichikama {{ill|Kirin Shishi-mai|ja|麒麟獅子舞|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kirin Shishi-mai|ja_comment=fixed jawiki redirect|3=麒麟獅子|1=Qilin dance|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11677329}} Dance''' - Town-designated Intangible Folk Cultural Property (designated March 31, 1986)<ref>{{Cite web|title=Shichikama Kirin Shishi-mai|url=https://www.town.shinonsen.hyogo.jp/page/?mode=detail&page_id=8ab359199e3f3c495d46db6ceb3791ec|access-date=2022-12-19|publisher=Shin'onsen Town}}</ref>
* '''Sangu Shrine Sacred Forest''' (山宮神社社叢) - Town-designated Natural Monument (designated March 31, 1986), featuring ancient [[:en:Castanopsis sieboldii|Castanopsis]] and [[:en:Chamaecyparis obtusa|Hinoki cypress]] trees, along with thick growths of {{ill|Camellia japonica|ja|ヤブツバキ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Camellia japonica|ja_comment=fixed jawiki redirect|3=ツバキ|1=Camellia japonica|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q160121}} and [[:en:Cleyera japonica|Sakaki]].<ref>{{Cite web|title=Sacred Forest of Shichikama Sangu Shrine|url=https://www.town.shinonsen.hyogo.jp/page/?mode=detail&page_id=b500773414376fa6bd4970ec294860e2|access-date=2022-12-19|publisher=Shin'onsen Town}}</ref>

== Access ==
* [[:en:West Japan Railway Company|West Japan Railway Company]] (JR West) [[:en:San'in Main Line|San'in Main Line]]: From [[:en:Hamasaka Station|Hamasaka Station]], take the {{ill|Zentan Bus|ja|全但バス|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zentan Bus|1=Zentan Bus|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11389110}} bound for {{ill|Yumura Onsen (Hyōgo)|ja|湯村温泉|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yumura Onsen (Hyōgo)|1=Yumura Onsen (Hyōgo)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11563591}}, and get off at "Shichikama Onsen-guchi" bus stop. 5-minute walk.
* From Osaka or Kobe, take the Zentan Bus {{ill|Yumechiyo-go|ja|夢千代号|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yumechiyo-go|ja_comment=fixed jawiki redirect|3=全但バス|1=Zentan Bus|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11389110}} to "Yumura Onsen," transfer to the bus for Hamasaka, and get off at "Shichikama Onsen-guchi." 5-minute walk.

'''Sangu Shrine''' (山宮神社, *Sangu Jinja*) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located at Shichikama, Shin'onsen, Mikata District, [[:en:Hyōgo Prefecture|Hyōgo Prefecture]]. It was formerly classified as a {{ill|Village shrine|ja|村社|1=Son-sha|WD=Q134917284}}{{efn|Former shrine rank|lt=Son-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Village shrine|ja_comment=fixed jawiki redirect|3=近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}. The main deity enshrined is {{ill|Ōyamatsumi|ja|大山祇命|1=Ōyamatsumi|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamatsumi|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamatsumi|ja_comment=fixed jawiki redirect|3=オオヤマツミ}}, with additional enshrinements of [[:en:Izanagi|Izanagi]], [[:en:Izanami|Izanami]], {{ill|Homuda Wake no Mikoto|ja|誉田別尊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Homuda Wake no Mikoto|ja_comment=fixed jawiki redirect|3=応神天皇|1=Ōjin|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q317997}}, and {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|スサノオノミコト|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=fixed jawiki redirect|3=スサノオ}}.

== Overview ==
The shrine is located atop a small forested hill near the source of the {{ill|Shichikama Onsen|ja|七釜温泉|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shichikama Onsen|1=Shichikama Onsen|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11353724}}. The exact founding date is unknown. Before the modern era, it was known as '''Sangami Gongen''' (山神権現). In [[:en:1868|1868]] (the first year of the [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]]), it was renamed to its current title. In [[:en:1873|1873]] (Meiji 6), it was designated a village shrine and enshrined [[:en:Hachiman|Hachiman]] and the local {{ill|Placenta deity|ja|胞衣神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Placenta deity|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.

The shrine's main hall was rebuilt in [[:en:1444|1444]] (Bun'an 1, [[:en:Muromachi period|Muromachi period]]), but was destroyed in [[:en:1944|1944]] (Shōwa 19) due to a typhoon. The current structure was rebuilt after World War II.

== Festivals and Events ==
* Annual Festival: September 29<ref>{{Cite web|title=Festival Calendar - Sangu Shrine|url=https://www.kirinnomachi-japan-heritage.jp/jp/reisai/1059/|access-date=2022-12-19|publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs}}</ref>

== Cultural Properties ==
* '''Shichikama {{ill|Kirin Shishi-mai|ja|麒麟獅子舞|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kirin Shishi-mai|ja_comment=fixed jawiki redirect|3=麒麟獅子|1=Qilin dance|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11677329}} Dance''' - Town-designated Intangible Folk Cultural Property (designated March 31, 1986)<ref>{{Cite web|title=Shichikama Kirin Shishi-mai|url=https://www.town.shinonsen.hyogo.jp/page/?mode=detail&page_id=8ab359199e3f3c495d46db6ceb3791ec|access-date=2022-12-19|publisher=Shin'onsen Town}}</ref>
* '''Sangu Shrine Sacred Forest''' (山宮神社社叢) - Town-designated Natural Monument (designated March 31, 1986), featuring ancient [[:en:Castanopsis sieboldii|Castanopsis]] and [[:en:Chamaecyparis obtusa|Hinoki cypress]] trees, along with thick growths of {{ill|Camellia japonica|ja|ヤブツバキ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Camellia japonica|ja_comment=fixed jawiki redirect|3=ツバキ|1=Camellia japonica|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q160121}} and [[:en:Cleyera japonica|Sakaki]].<ref>{{Cite web|title=Sacred Forest of Shichikama Sangu Shrine|url=https://www.town.shinonsen.hyogo.jp/page/?mode=detail&page_id=b500773414376fa6bd4970ec294860e2|access-date=2022-12-19|publisher=Shin'onsen Town}}</ref>

== Access ==
* [[:en:West Japan Railway Company|West Japan Railway Company]] (JR West) [[:en:San'in Main Line|San'in Main Line]]: From [[:en:Hamasaka Station|Hamasaka Station]], take the {{ill|Zentan Bus|ja|全但バス|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zentan Bus|1=Zentan Bus|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11389110}} bound for {{ill|Yumura Onsen (Hyōgo)|ja|湯村温泉|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yumura Onsen (Hyōgo)|1=Yumura Onsen (Hyōgo)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11563591}}, and get off at "Shichikama Onsen-guchi" bus stop. 5-minute walk.
* From Osaka or Kobe, take the Zentan Bus {{ill|Yumechiyo-go|ja|夢千代号|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yumechiyo-go|ja_comment=fixed jawiki redirect|3=全但バス|1=Zentan Bus|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11389110}} to "Yumura Onsen," transfer to the bus for Hamasaka, and get off at "Shichikama Onsen-guchi." 5-minute walk.

** {{ill|Hamasaka Onsen|ja|浜坂温泉|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hamasaka Onsen|1=Hamasaka Onsen|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11557617}} (9-minute bus ride)
* {{ill|Yumura Onsen (Hyōgo)|ja|湯村温泉|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yumura Onsen (Hyōgo)|1=Yumura Onsen (Hyōgo)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11563591}} (15-minute bus ride)
* {{ill|Hamasaka Prefectural Sun Beach|ja|浜坂県民サンビーチ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hamasaka Prefectural Sun Beach|1=Hamasaka Kenmin Sun　Beach|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11557620}}
* {{ill|Tajima Mihonoura|ja|但馬御火浦|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tajima Mihonoura|1=Tajima Mihonoura|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11381630}}

* Annual Festival: September 29<ref>{{Cite web|title=Festival Calendar - Sangu Shrine|url=https://www.kirinnomachi-japan-heritage.jp/jp/reisai/1059/|access-date=2022-12-19|publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs}}</ref>

* '''Sangu Shrine Sacred Forest''' (山宮神社社叢) - Town-designated Natural Monument (designated March 31, 1986), featuring ancient [[:en:Castanopsis sieboldii|Castanopsis]] and [[:en:Chamaecyparis obtusa|Hinoki cypress]] trees, along with thick growths of {{ill|Camellia japonica|ja|ヤブツバキ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Camellia japonica|ja_comment=fixed jawiki redirect|3=ツバキ|1=Camellia japonica|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q160121}} and [[:en:Cleyera japonica|Sakaki]].<ref>{{Cite web|title=Sacred Forest of Shichikama Sangu Shrine|url=https://www.town.shinonsen.hyogo.jp/page/?mode=detail&page_id=b500773414376fa6bd4970ec294860e2|access-date=2022-12-19|publisher=Shin'onsen Town}}</ref>

'''Sangu Shrine''' (山宮神社, *Sangu Jinja*) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located at Shichikama, Shin'onsen, Mikata District, [[:en:Hyōgo Prefecture|Hyōgo Prefecture]]. It was formerly classified as a {{ill|Village shrine|ja|村社|1=Son-sha|WD=Q134917284}}{{efn|Former shrine rank|lt=Son-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Village shrine|ja_comment=fixed jawiki redirect|3=近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}. The main deity enshrined is {{ill|Ōyamatsumi|ja|大山祇命|1=Ōyamatsumi|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamatsumi|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamatsumi|ja_comment=fixed jawiki redirect|3=オオヤマツミ}}, with additional enshrinements of [[:en:Izanagi|Izanagi]], [[:en:Izanami|Izanami]], {{ill|Homuda Wake no Mikoto|ja|誉田別尊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Homuda Wake no Mikoto|ja_comment=fixed jawiki redirect|3=応神天皇|1=Ōjin|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q317997}}, and {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|スサノオノミコト|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=fixed jawiki redirect|3=スサノオ}}.

== Overview ==
The shrine is located atop a small forested hill near the source of the {{ill|Shichikama Onsen|ja|七釜温泉|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shichikama Onsen|1=Shichikama Onsen|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11353724}}. The exact founding date is unknown. Before the modern era, it was known as '''Sangami Gongen''' (山神権現). In [[:en:1868|1868]] (the first year of the [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]]), it was renamed to its current title. In [[:en:1873|1873]] (Meiji 6), it was designated a village shrine and enshrined [[:en:Hachiman|Hachiman]] and the local {{ill|Placenta deity|ja|胞衣神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Placenta deity|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.

The shrine's main hall was rebuilt in [[:en:1444|1444]] (Bun'an 1, [[:en:Muromachi period|Muromachi period]]), but was destroyed in [[:en:1944|1944]] (Shōwa 19) due to a typhoon. The current structure was rebuilt after World War II.

== Festivals and Events ==
* Annual Festival: September 29<ref>{{Cite web|title=Festival Calendar - Sangu Shrine|url=https://www.kirinnomachi-japan-heritage.jp/jp/reisai/1059/|access-date=2022-12-19|publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs}}</ref>

== Cultural Properties ==

* '''Sangu Shrine Sacred Forest''' (山宮神社社叢) - Town-designated Natural Monument (designated March 31, 1986), featuring ancient [[:en:Castanopsis sieboldii|Castanopsis]] and [[:en:Chamaecyparis obtusa|Hinoki cypress]] trees, along with thick growths of {{ill|Camellia japonica|ja|ヤブツバキ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Camellia japonica|ja_comment=fixed jawiki redirect|3=ツバキ|1=Camellia japonica|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q160121}} and [[:en:Cleyera japonica|Sakaki]].<ref>{{Cite web|title=Sacred Forest of Shichikama Sangu Shrine|url=https://www.town.shinonsen.hyogo.jp/page/?mode=detail&page_id=b500773414376fa6bd4970ec294860e2|access-date=2022-12-19|publisher=Shin'onsen Town}}</ref>

'''Sangu Shrine''' (山宮神社, *Sangu Jinja*) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located at Shichikama, Shin'onsen, Mikata District, [[:en:Hyōgo Prefecture|Hyōgo Prefecture]]. It was formerly classified as a {{ill|Village shrine|ja|村社|1=Son-sha|WD=Q134917284}}{{efn|Former shrine rank|lt=Son-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Village shrine|ja_comment=fixed jawiki redirect|3=近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}. The main deity enshrined is {{ill|Ōyamatsumi|ja|大山祇命|1=Ōyamatsumi|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamatsumi|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamatsumi|ja_comment=fixed jawiki redirect|3=オオヤマツミ}}, with additional enshrinements of [[:en:Izanagi|Izanagi]], [[:en:Izanami|Izanami]], {{ill|Homuda Wake no Mikoto|ja|誉田別尊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Homuda Wake no Mikoto|ja_comment=fixed jawiki redirect|3=応神天皇|1=Ōjin|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q317997}}, and {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|スサノオノミコト|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=fixed jawiki redirect|3=スサノオ}}.

== Overview ==
The shrine is located atop a small forested hill near the source of the {{ill|Shichikama Onsen|ja|七釜温泉|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shichikama Onsen|1=Shichikama Onsen|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11353724}}. The exact founding date is unknown. Before the modern era, it was known as '''Sangami Gongen''' (山神権現). In [[:en:1868|1868]] (the first year of the [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]]), it was renamed to its current title. In [[:en:1873|1873]] (Meiji 6), it was designated a village shrine and enshrined [[:en:Hachiman|Hachiman]] and the local {{ill|Placenta deity|ja|胞衣神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Placenta deity|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.

The shrine's main hall was rebuilt in [[:en:1444|1444]] (Bun'an 1, [[:en:Muromachi period|Muromachi period]]), but was destroyed in [[:en:1944|1944]] (Shōwa 19) due to a typhoon. The current structure was rebuilt after World War II.

== Festivals and Events ==
* Annual Festival: September 29<ref>{{Cite web|title=Festival Calendar - Sangu Shrine|url=https://www.kirinnomachi-japan-heritage.jp/jp/reisai/1059/|access-date=2022-12-19|publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs}}</ref>

== Cultural Properties ==
* '''Shichikama {{ill|Kirin Shishi-mai|ja|麒麟獅子舞|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kirin Shishi-mai|ja_comment=fixed jawiki redirect|3=麒麟獅子|1=Qilin dance|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11677329}} Dance''' - Town-designated Intangible Folk Cultural Property (designated March 31, 1986)<ref>{{Cite web|title=Shichikama Kirin Shishi-mai|url=https://www.town.shinonsen.hyogo.jp/page/?mode=detail&page_id=8ab359199e3f3c495d46db6ceb3791ec|access-date=2022-12-19|publisher=Shin'onsen Town}}</ref>
* '''Sangu Shrine Sacred Forest''' (山宮神社社叢) - Town-designated Natural Monument (designated March 31, 1986), featuring ancient [[:en:Castanopsis sieboldii|Castanopsis]] and [[:en:Chamaecyparis obtusa|Hinoki cypress]] trees, along with thick growths of {{ill|Camellia japonica|ja|ヤブツバキ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Camellia japonica|ja_comment=fixed jawiki redirect|3=ツバキ|1=Camellia japonica|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q160121}} and [[:en:Cleyera japonica|Sakaki]].<ref>{{Cite web|title=Sacred Forest of Shichikama Sangu Shrine|url=https://www.town.shinonsen.hyogo.jp/page/?mode=detail&page_id=b500773414376fa6bd4970ec294860e2|access-date=2022-12-19|publisher=Shin'onsen Town}}</ref>

== Access ==
* [[:en:West Japan Railway Company|West Japan Railway Company]] (JR West) [[:en:San'in Main Line|San'in Main Line]]: From [[:en:Hamasaka Station|Hamasaka Station]], take the {{ill|Zentan Bus|ja|全但バス|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zentan Bus|1=Zentan Bus|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11389110}} bound for {{ill|Yumura Onsen (Hyōgo)|ja|湯村温泉|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yumura Onsen (Hyōgo)|1=Yumura Onsen (Hyōgo)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11563591}}, and get off at "Shichikama Onsen-guchi" bus stop. 5-minute walk.
* From Osaka or Kobe, take the Zentan Bus {{ill|Yumechiyo-go|ja|夢千代号|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yumechiyo-go|ja_comment=fixed jawiki redirect|3=全但バス|1=Zentan Bus|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11389110}} to "Yumura Onsen," transfer to the bus for Hamasaka, and get off at "Shichikama Onsen-guchi." 5-minute walk.

== Nearby Attractions ==
* {{ill|Hamasaka Onsenkyō|ja|浜坂温泉郷|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hamasaka Onsenkyō|1=Hamasaka Onsenkyō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11557619}}
** {{ill|Hamasaka Onsen|ja|浜坂温泉|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hamasaka Onsen|1=Hamasaka Onsen|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11557617}} (9-minute bus ride)
* {{ill|Yumura Onsen (Hyōgo)|ja|湯村温泉|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yumura Onsen (Hyōgo)|1=Yumura Onsen (Hyōgo)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11563591}} (15-minute bus ride)
* {{ill|Hamasaka Prefectural Sun Beach|ja|浜坂県民サンビーチ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hamasaka Prefectural Sun Beach|1=Hamasaka Kenmin Sun　Beach|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11557620}}
* {{ill|Tajima Mihonoura|ja|但馬御火浦|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tajima Mihonoura|1=Tajima Mihonoura|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11381630}}

== Access == * [[:en:West Japan Railway Company|West Japan Railway Company]] (JR West) [[:en:San'in Main Line|San'in Main Line]]: From [[:en:Hamasaka Station|Hamasaka Station]], take the {{ill|Zentan Bus|ja|全但バス|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zentan Bus|1=Zentan Bus|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11389110}} bound for {{ill|Yumura Onsen (Hyōgo)|ja|湯村温泉|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yumura Onsen (Hyōgo)|1=Yumura Onsen (Hyōgo)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11563591}}, and get off at "Shichikama Onsen-guchi" bus stop. 5-minute walk. * From Osaka or Kobe, take the Zentan Bus {{ill|Yumechiyo-go|ja|夢千代号|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yumechiyo-go|ja_comment=fixed jawiki redirect|3=全但バス|1=Zentan Bus|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11389110}} to "Yumura Onsen," transfer to the bus for Hamasaka, and get off at "Shichikama Onsen-guchi." 5-minute walk.
== Nearby Attractions == * {{ill|Hamasaka Onsenkyō|ja|浜坂温泉郷|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hamasaka Onsenkyō|1=Hamasaka Onsenkyō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11557619}} ** {{ill|Hamasaka Onsen|ja|浜坂温泉|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hamasaka Onsen|1=Hamasaka Onsen|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11557617}} (9-minute bus ride) * {{ill|Yumura Onsen (Hyōgo)|ja|湯村温泉|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yumura Onsen (Hyōgo)|1=Yumura Onsen (Hyōgo)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11563591}} (15-minute bus ride) * {{ill|Hamasaka Prefectural Sun Beach|ja|浜坂県民サンビーチ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hamasaka Prefectural Sun Beach|1=Hamasaka Kenmin Sun Beach|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11557620}} * {{ill|Tajima Mihonoura|ja|但馬御火浦|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tajima Mihonoura|1=Tajima Mihonoura|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11381630}}

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:山宮神社]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[Category:Has wikidata]][[d:Q11467748]] Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 05:33, 12 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:25, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | Name = Sangu Shrine | image = [[File:Sangu-jinja Shinonsen03s5s4592.jpg|300px]] | location = [[:en:Hyōgo Prefecture|Hyōgo Prefecture]], [[:en:Mikata District, Hyōgo|Mikata District, Hyōgo]], [[:en:Shin'onsen, Hyōgo|Shin'onsen]], Shichikama 1409 | coordinates = {{coord|35|36|39.6|N|134|28|44.7|E|region:JP-28_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | deity = {{ill|Ōyamatsumi|ja|大山祇命|1=Ōyamatsumi|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamatsumi|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamatsumi|ja_comment=fixed jawiki redirect|3=オオヤマツミ}} | Type = Village Shrine (Former) | Established = Unknown | Main Hall = | Alternate Name = | Annual Festival = September 29 | Rituals = | Map = {{location map|Japan Hyōgo|lat_deg=35|lat_min=36|lat_sec=39.6|lon_deg=134|lon_min=28|lon_sec=44.7|label=Sangu Shrine|position=center|width=200|float=center}} }}
'''Sangu Shrine''' (山宮神社, *Sangu Jinja*) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located at Shichikama, Shin'onsen, Mikata District, [[:en:Hyōgo Prefecture|Hyōgo Prefecture]]. It was formerly classified as a {{ill|Village shrine|ja|村社|1=Son-sha|WD=Q134917284}}{{efn|Former shrine rank|lt=Son-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Village shrine|ja_comment=fixed jawiki redirect|3=近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}. The main deity enshrined is {{ill|Ōyamatsumi|ja|大山祇命|1=Ōyamatsumi|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamatsumi|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamatsumi|ja_comment=fixed jawiki redirect|3=オオヤマツミ}}, with additional enshrinements of [[:en:Izanagi|Izanagi]], [[:en:Izanami|Izanami]], {{ill|Homuda Wake no Mikoto|ja|誉田別尊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Homuda Wake no Mikoto|ja_comment=fixed jawiki redirect|3=応神天皇|1=Ōjin|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q317997}}, and {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|スサノオノミコト|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=fixed jawiki redirect|3=スサノオ}}.
== Overview == The shrine is located atop a small forested hill near the source of the {{ill|Shichikama Onsen|ja|七釜温泉|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shichikama Onsen|1=Shichikama Onsen|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11353724}}. The exact founding date is unknown. Before the modern era, it was known as '''Sangami Gongen''' (山神権現). In [[:en:1868|1868]] (the first year of the [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]]), it was renamed to its current title. In [[:en:1873|1873]] (Meiji 6), it was designated a village shrine and enshrined [[:en:Hachiman|Hachiman]] and the local {{ill|Placenta deity|ja|胞衣神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Placenta deity|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.
The shrine's main hall was rebuilt in [[:en:1444|1444]] (Bun'an 1, [[:en:Muromachi period|Muromachi period]]), but was destroyed in [[:en:1944|1944]] (Shōwa 19) due to a typhoon. The current structure was rebuilt after World War II.
== Festivals and Events == * Annual Festival: September 29<ref>{{Cite web|title=Festival Calendar - Sangu Shrine|url=https://www.kirinnomachi-japan-heritage.jp/jp/reisai/1059/|access-date=2022-12-19|publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs}}</ref>
== Cultural Properties == * '''Shichikama {{ill|Kirin Shishi-mai|ja|麒麟獅子舞|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kirin Shishi-mai|ja_comment=fixed jawiki redirect|3=麒麟獅子|1=Qilin dance|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11677329}} Dance''' - Town-designated Intangible Folk Cultural Property (designated March 31, 1986)<ref>{{Cite web|title=Shichikama Kirin Shishi-mai|url=https://www.town.shinonsen.hyogo.jp/page/?mode=detail&page_id=8ab359199e3f3c495d46db6ceb3791ec|access-date=2022-12-19|publisher=Shin'onsen Town}}</ref> * '''Sangu Shrine Sacred Forest''' (山宮神社社叢) - Town-designated Natural Monument (designated March 31, 1986), featuring ancient [[:en:Castanopsis sieboldii|Castanopsis]] and [[:en:Chamaecyparis obtusa|Hinoki cypress]] trees, along with thick growths of {{ill|Camellia japonica|ja|ヤブツバキ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Camellia japonica|ja_comment=fixed jawiki redirect|3=ツバキ|1=Camellia japonica|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q160121}} and [[:en:Cleyera japonica|Sakaki]].<ref>{{Cite web|title=Sacred Forest of Shichikama Sangu Shrine|url=https://www.town.shinonsen.hyogo.jp/page/?mode=detail&page_id=b500773414376fa6bd4970ec294860e2|access-date=2022-12-19|publisher=Shin'onsen Town}}</ref>
== Access == * [[:en:West Japan Railway Company|West Japan Railway Company]] (JR West) [[:en:San'in Main Line|San'in Main Line]]: From [[:en:Hamasaka Station|Hamasaka Station]], take the {{ill|Zentan Bus|ja|全但バス|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zentan Bus|1=Zentan Bus|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11389110}} bound for {{ill|Yumura Onsen (Hyōgo)|ja|湯村温泉|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yumura Onsen (Hyōgo)|1=Yumura Onsen (Hyōgo)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11563591}}, and get off at "Shichikama Onsen-guchi" bus stop. 5-minute walk. * From Osaka or Kobe, take the Zentan Bus {{ill|Yumechiyo-go|ja|夢千代号|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yumechiyo-go|ja_comment=fixed jawiki redirect|3=全但バス|1=Zentan Bus|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11389110}} to "Yumura Onsen," transfer to the bus for Hamasaka, and get off at "Shichikama Onsen-guchi." 5-minute walk.
== Nearby Attractions == * {{ill|Hamasaka Onsenkyō|ja|浜坂温泉郷|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hamasaka Onsenkyō|1=Hamasaka Onsenkyō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11557619}} ** {{ill|Hamasaka Onsen|ja|浜坂温泉|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hamasaka Onsen|1=Hamasaka Onsen|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11557617}} (9-minute bus ride) * {{ill|Yumura Onsen (Hyōgo)|ja|湯村温泉|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yumura Onsen (Hyōgo)|1=Yumura Onsen (Hyōgo)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11563591}} (15-minute bus ride) * {{ill|Hamasaka Prefectural Sun Beach|ja|浜坂県民サンビーチ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hamasaka Prefectural Sun Beach|1=Hamasaka Kenmin Sun Beach|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11557620}} * {{ill|Tajima Mihonoura|ja|但馬御火浦|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tajima Mihonoura|1=Tajima Mihonoura|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11381630}}

'''Sangu Shrine''' (山宮神社, *Sangu Jinja*) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located at Shichikama, Shin'onsen, Mikata District, [[:en:Hyōgo Prefecture|Hyōgo Prefecture]]. It was formerly classified as a {{ill|Village shrine|ja|村社|1=Son-sha|WD=Q134917284}}{{efn|Former shrine rank|lt=Son-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Village shrine|ja_comment=fixed jawiki redirect|3=近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}. The main deity enshrined is {{ill|Ōyamatsumi|ja|大山祇命|1=Ōyamatsumi|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamatsumi|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamatsumi|ja_comment=fixed jawiki redirect|3=オオヤマツミ}}, with additional enshrinements of [[:en:Izanagi|Izanagi]], [[:en:Izanami|Izanami]], {{ill|Homuda Wake no Mikoto|ja|誉田別尊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Homuda Wake no Mikoto|ja_comment=fixed jawiki redirect|3=応神天皇|1=Ōjin|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q317997}}, and {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|スサノオノミコト|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=fixed jawiki redirect|3=スサノオ}}.

== Overview ==
The shrine is located atop a small forested hill near the source of the {{ill|Shichikama Onsen|ja|七釜温泉|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shichikama Onsen|1=Shichikama Onsen|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11353724}}. The exact founding date is unknown. Before the modern era, it was known as '''Sangami Gongen''' (山神権現). In [[:en:1868|1868]] (the first year of the [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]]), it was renamed to its current title. In [[:en:1873|1873]] (Meiji 6), it was designated a village shrine and enshrined [[:en:Hachiman|Hachiman]] and the local {{ill|Placenta deity|ja|胞衣神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Placenta deity|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.

The shrine's main hall was rebuilt in [[:en:1444|1444]] (Bun'an 1, [[:en:Muromachi period|Muromachi period]]), but was destroyed in [[:en:1944|1944]] (Shōwa 19) due to a typhoon. The current structure was rebuilt after World War II.

== Festivals and Events ==
* Annual Festival: September 29<ref>{{Cite web|title=Festival Calendar - Sangu Shrine|url=https://www.kirinnomachi-japan-heritage.jp/jp/reisai/1059/|access-date=2022-12-19|publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs}}</ref>

== Cultural Properties ==
* '''Shichikama {{ill|Kirin Shishi-mai|ja|麒麟獅子舞|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kirin Shishi-mai|ja_comment=fixed jawiki redirect|3=麒麟獅子|1=Qilin dance|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11677329}} Dance''' - Town-designated Intangible Folk Cultural Property (designated March 31, 1986)<ref>{{Cite web|title=Shichikama Kirin Shishi-mai|url=https://www.town.shinonsen.hyogo.jp/page/?mode=detail&page_id=8ab359199e3f3c495d46db6ceb3791ec|access-date=2022-12-19|publisher=Shin'onsen Town}}</ref>
* '''Sangu Shrine Sacred Forest''' (山宮神社社叢) - Town-designated Natural Monument (designated March 31, 1986), featuring ancient [[:en:Castanopsis sieboldii|Castanopsis]] and [[:en:Chamaecyparis obtusa|Hinoki cypress]] trees, along with thick growths of {{ill|Camellia japonica|ja|ヤブツバキ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Camellia japonica|ja_comment=fixed jawiki redirect|3=ツバキ|1=Camellia japonica|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q160121}} and [[:en:Cleyera japonica|Sakaki]].<ref>{{Cite web|title=Sacred Forest of Shichikama Sangu Shrine|url=https://www.town.shinonsen.hyogo.jp/page/?mode=detail&page_id=b500773414376fa6bd4970ec294860e2|access-date=2022-12-19|publisher=Shin'onsen Town}}</ref>

== Access ==
* [[:en:West Japan Railway Company|West Japan Railway Company]] (JR West) [[:en:San'in Main Line|San'in Main Line]]: From [[:en:Hamasaka Station|Hamasaka Station]], take the {{ill|Zentan Bus|ja|全但バス|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zentan Bus|1=Zentan Bus|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11389110}} bound for {{ill|Yumura Onsen (Hyōgo)|ja|湯村温泉|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yumura Onsen (Hyōgo)|1=Yumura Onsen (Hyōgo)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11563591}}, and get off at "Shichikama Onsen-guchi" bus stop. 5-minute walk.
* From Osaka or Kobe, take the Zentan Bus {{ill|Yumechiyo-go|ja|夢千代号|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yumechiyo-go|ja_comment=fixed jawiki redirect|3=全但バス|1=Zentan Bus|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11389110}} to "Yumura Onsen," transfer to the bus for Hamasaka, and get off at "Shichikama Onsen-guchi." 5-minute walk.

== Nearby Attractions ==
* {{ill|Hamasaka Onsenkyō|ja|浜坂温泉郷|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hamasaka Onsenkyō|1=Hamasaka Onsenkyō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11557619}}
** {{ill|Hamasaka Onsen|ja|浜坂温泉|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hamasaka Onsen|1=Hamasaka Onsen|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11557617}} (9-minute bus ride)
* {{ill|Yumura Onsen (Hyōgo)|ja|湯村温泉|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yumura Onsen (Hyōgo)|1=Yumura Onsen (Hyōgo)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11563591}} (15-minute bus ride)
* {{ill|Hamasaka Prefectural Sun Beach|ja|浜坂県民サンビーチ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hamasaka Prefectural Sun Beach|1=Hamasaka Kenmin Sun　Beach|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11557620}}
* {{ill|Tajima Mihonoura|ja|但馬御火浦|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tajima Mihonoura|1=Tajima Mihonoura|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11381630}}

'''Sangu Shrine''' (山宮神社, *Sangu Jinja*) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located at Shichikama, Shin'onsen, Mikata District, [[:en:Hyōgo Prefecture|Hyōgo Prefecture]]. It was formerly classified as a {{ill|Village shrine|ja|村社|1=Son-sha|WD=Q134917284}}{{efn|Former shrine rank|lt=Son-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Village shrine|ja_comment=fixed jawiki redirect|3=近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}. The main deity enshrined is {{ill|Ōyamatsumi|ja|大山祇命|1=Ōyamatsumi|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamatsumi|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamatsumi|ja_comment=fixed jawiki redirect|3=オオヤマツミ}}, with additional enshrinements of [[:en:Izanagi|Izanagi]], [[:en:Izanami|Izanami]], {{ill|Homuda Wake no Mikoto|ja|誉田別尊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Homuda Wake no Mikoto|ja_comment=fixed jawiki redirect|3=応神天皇|1=Ōjin|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q317997}}, and {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|スサノオノミコト|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=fixed jawiki redirect|3=スサノオ}}.
== Overview == The shrine is located atop a small forested hill near the source of the {{ill|Shichikama Onsen|ja|七釜温泉|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shichikama Onsen|1=Shichikama Onsen|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11353724}}. The exact founding date is unknown. Before the modern era, it was known as '''Sangami Gongen''' (山神権現). In [[:en:1868|1868]] (the first year of the [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]]), it was renamed to its current title. In [[:en:1873|1873]] (Meiji 6), it was designated a village shrine and enshrined [[:en:Hachiman|Hachiman]] and the local {{ill|Placenta deity|ja|胞衣神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Placenta deity|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.
The shrine's main hall was rebuilt in [[:en:1444|1444]] (Bun'an 1, [[:en:Muromachi period|Muromachi period]]), but was destroyed in [[:en:1944|1944]] (Shōwa 19) due to a typhoon. The current structure was rebuilt after World War II.
== Festivals and Events == * Annual Festival: September 29<ref>{{Cite web|title=Festival Calendar - Sangu Shrine|url=https://www.kirinnomachi-japan-heritage.jp/jp/reisai/1059/|access-date=2022-12-19|publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs}}</ref>
== Cultural Properties == * '''Shichikama {{ill|Kirin Shishi-mai|ja|麒麟獅子舞|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kirin Shishi-mai|ja_comment=fixed jawiki redirect|3=麒麟獅子|1=Qilin dance|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11677329}} Dance''' - Town-designated Intangible Folk Cultural Property (designated March 31, 1986)<ref>{{Cite web|title=Shichikama Kirin Shishi-mai|url=https://www.town.shinonsen.hyogo.jp/page/?mode=detail&page_id=8ab359199e3f3c495d46db6ceb3791ec|access-date=2022-12-19|publisher=Shin'onsen Town}}</ref> * '''Sangu Shrine Sacred Forest''' (山宮神社社叢) - Town-designated Natural Monument (designated March 31, 1986), featuring ancient [[:en:Castanopsis sieboldii|Castanopsis]] and [[:en:Chamaecyparis obtusa|Hinoki cypress]] trees, along with thick growths of {{ill|Camellia japonica|ja|ヤブツバキ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Camellia japonica|ja_comment=fixed jawiki redirect|3=ツバキ|1=Camellia japonica|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q160121}} and [[:en:Cleyera japonica|Sakaki]].<ref>{{Cite web|title=Sacred Forest of Shichikama Sangu Shrine|url=https://www.town.shinonsen.hyogo.jp/page/?mode=detail&page_id=b500773414376fa6bd4970ec294860e2|access-date=2022-12-19|publisher=Shin'onsen Town}}</ref>
== Access == * [[:en:West Japan Railway Company|West Japan Railway Company]] (JR West) [[:en:San'in Main Line|San'in Main Line]]: From [[:en:Hamasaka Station|Hamasaka Station]], take the {{ill|Zentan Bus|ja|全但バス|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zentan Bus|1=Zentan Bus|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11389110}} bound for {{ill|Yumura Onsen (Hyōgo)|ja|湯村温泉|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yumura Onsen (Hyōgo)|1=Yumura Onsen (Hyōgo)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11563591}}, and get off at "Shichikama Onsen-guchi" bus stop. 5-minute walk. * From Osaka or Kobe, take the Zentan Bus {{ill|Yumechiyo-go|ja|夢千代号|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yumechiyo-go|ja_comment=fixed jawiki redirect|3=全但バス|1=Zentan Bus|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11389110}} to "Yumura Onsen," transfer to the bus for Hamasaka, and get off at "Shichikama Onsen-guchi." 5-minute walk.
== Nearby Attractions == * {{ill|Hamasaka Onsenkyō|ja|浜坂温泉郷|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hamasaka Onsenkyō|1=Hamasaka Onsenkyō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11557619}} ** {{ill|Hamasaka Onsen|ja|浜坂温泉|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hamasaka Onsen|1=Hamasaka Onsen|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11557617}} (9-minute bus ride) * {{ill|Yumura Onsen (Hyōgo)|ja|湯村温泉|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yumura Onsen (Hyōgo)|1=Yumura Onsen (Hyōgo)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11563591}} (15-minute bus ride) * {{ill|Hamasaka Prefectural Sun Beach|ja|浜坂県民サンビーチ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hamasaka Prefectural Sun Beach|1=Hamasaka Kenmin Sun Beach|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11557620}} * {{ill|Tajima Mihonoura|ja|但馬御火浦|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tajima Mihonoura|1=Tajima Mihonoura|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11381630}}

'''Sangu Shrine''' (山宮神社, *Sangu Jinja*) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located at Shichikama, Shin'onsen, Mikata District, [[:en:Hyōgo Prefecture|Hyōgo Prefecture]]. It was formerly classified as a {{ill|Village shrine|ja|村社|1=Son-sha|WD=Q134917284}}{{efn|Former shrine rank|lt=Son-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Village shrine|ja_comment=fixed jawiki redirect|3=近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}. The main deity enshrined is {{ill|Ōyamatsumi|ja|大山祇命|1=Ōyamatsumi|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamatsumi|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamatsumi|ja_comment=fixed jawiki redirect|3=オオヤマツミ}}, with additional enshrinements of [[:en:Izanagi|Izanagi]], [[:en:Izanami|Izanami]], {{ill|Homuda Wake no Mikoto|ja|誉田別尊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Homuda Wake no Mikoto|ja_comment=fixed jawiki redirect|3=応神天皇|1=Ōjin|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q317997}}, and {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|スサノオノミコト|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=fixed jawiki redirect|3=スサノオ}}.

== Overview ==
The shrine is located atop a small forested hill near the source of the {{ill|Shichikama Onsen|ja|七釜温泉|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shichikama Onsen|1=Shichikama Onsen|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11353724}}. The exact founding date is unknown. Before the modern era, it was known as '''Sangami Gongen''' (山神権現). In [[:en:1868|1868]] (the first year of the [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]]), it was renamed to its current title. In [[:en:1873|1873]] (Meiji 6), it was designated a village shrine and enshrined [[:en:Hachiman|Hachiman]] and the local {{ill|Placenta deity|ja|胞衣神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Placenta deity|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.

The shrine's main hall was rebuilt in [[:en:1444|1444]] (Bun'an 1, [[:en:Muromachi period|Muromachi period]]), but was destroyed in [[:en:1944|1944]] (Shōwa 19) due to a typhoon. The current structure was rebuilt after World War II.

== Festivals and Events ==
* Annual Festival: September 29<ref>{{Cite web|title=Festival Calendar - Sangu Shrine|url=https://www.kirinnomachi-japan-heritage.jp/jp/reisai/1059/|access-date=2022-12-19|publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs}}</ref>

== Cultural Properties ==
* '''Shichikama {{ill|Kirin Shishi-mai|ja|麒麟獅子舞|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kirin Shishi-mai|ja_comment=fixed jawiki redirect|3=麒麟獅子|1=Qilin dance|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11677329}} Dance''' - Town-designated Intangible Folk Cultural Property (designated March 31, 1986)<ref>{{Cite web|title=Shichikama Kirin Shishi-mai|url=https://www.town.shinonsen.hyogo.jp/page/?mode=detail&page_id=8ab359199e3f3c495d46db6ceb3791ec|access-date=2022-12-19|publisher=Shin'onsen Town}}</ref>
* '''Sangu Shrine Sacred Forest''' (山宮神社社叢) - Town-designated Natural Monument (designated March 31, 1986), featuring ancient [[:en:Castanopsis sieboldii|Castanopsis]] and [[:en:Chamaecyparis obtusa|Hinoki cypress]] trees, along with thick growths of {{ill|Camellia japonica|ja|ヤブツバキ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Camellia japonica|ja_comment=fixed jawiki redirect|3=ツバキ|1=Camellia japonica|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q160121}} and [[:en:Cleyera japonica|Sakaki]].<ref>{{Cite web|title=Sacred Forest of Shichikama Sangu Shrine|url=https://www.town.shinonsen.hyogo.jp/page/?mode=detail&page_id=b500773414376fa6bd4970ec294860e2|access-date=2022-12-19|publisher=Shin'onsen Town}}</ref>

== Access ==
* [[:en:West Japan Railway Company|West Japan Railway Company]] (JR West) [[:en:San'in Main Line|San'in Main Line]]: From [[:en:Hamasaka Station|Hamasaka Station]], take the {{ill|Zentan Bus|ja|全但バス|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zentan Bus|1=Zentan Bus|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11389110}} bound for {{ill|Yumura Onsen (Hyōgo)|ja|湯村温泉|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yumura Onsen (Hyōgo)|1=Yumura Onsen (Hyōgo)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11563591}}, and get off at "Shichikama Onsen-guchi" bus stop. 5-minute walk.
* From Osaka or Kobe, take the Zentan Bus {{ill|Yumechiyo-go|ja|夢千代号|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yumechiyo-go|ja_comment=fixed jawiki redirect|3=全但バス|1=Zentan Bus|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11389110}} to "Yumura Onsen," transfer to the bus for Hamasaka, and get off at "Shichikama Onsen-guchi." 5-minute walk.

== Nearby Attractions ==

** {{ill|Hamasaka Onsen|ja|浜坂温泉|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hamasaka Onsen|1=Hamasaka Onsen|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11557617}} (9-minute bus ride)
* {{ill|Yumura Onsen (Hyōgo)|ja|湯村温泉|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yumura Onsen (Hyōgo)|1=Yumura Onsen (Hyōgo)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11563591}} (15-minute bus ride)
* {{ill|Hamasaka Prefectural Sun Beach|ja|浜坂県民サンビーチ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hamasaka Prefectural Sun Beach|1=Hamasaka Kenmin Sun　Beach|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11557620}}
* {{ill|Tajima Mihonoura|ja|但馬御火浦|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tajima Mihonoura|1=Tajima Mihonoura|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11381630}}

'''Sangu Shrine''' (山宮神社, *Sangu Jinja*) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located at Shichikama, Shin'onsen, Mikata District, [[:en:Hyōgo Prefecture|Hyōgo Prefecture]]. It was formerly classified as a {{ill|Village shrine|ja|村社|1=Son-sha|WD=Q134917284}}{{efn|Former shrine rank|lt=Son-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Village shrine|ja_comment=fixed jawiki redirect|3=近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}. The main deity enshrined is {{ill|Ōyamatsumi|ja|大山祇命|1=Ōyamatsumi|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamatsumi|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamatsumi|ja_comment=fixed jawiki redirect|3=オオヤマツミ}}, with additional enshrinements of [[:en:Izanagi|Izanagi]], [[:en:Izanami|Izanami]], {{ill|Homuda Wake no Mikoto|ja|誉田別尊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Homuda Wake no Mikoto|ja_comment=fixed jawiki redirect|3=応神天皇|1=Ōjin|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q317997}}, and {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|スサノオノミコト|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=fixed jawiki redirect|3=スサノオ}}.

== Overview ==
The shrine is located atop a small forested hill near the source of the {{ill|Shichikama Onsen|ja|七釜温泉|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shichikama Onsen|1=Shichikama Onsen|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11353724}}. The exact founding date is unknown. Before the modern era, it was known as '''Sangami Gongen''' (山神権現). In [[:en:1868|1868]] (the first year of the [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]]), it was renamed to its current title. In [[:en:1873|1873]] (Meiji 6), it was designated a village shrine and enshrined [[:en:Hachiman|Hachiman]] and the local {{ill|Placenta deity|ja|胞衣神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Placenta deity|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.

The shrine's main hall was rebuilt in [[:en:1444|1444]] (Bun'an 1, [[:en:Muromachi period|Muromachi period]]), but was destroyed in [[:en:1944|1944]] (Shōwa 19) due to a typhoon. The current structure was rebuilt after World War II.

== Festivals and Events ==
* Annual Festival: September 29<ref>{{Cite web|title=Festival Calendar - Sangu Shrine|url=https://www.kirinnomachi-japan-heritage.jp/jp/reisai/1059/|access-date=2022-12-19|publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs}}</ref>

== Cultural Properties ==
* '''Shichikama {{ill|Kirin Shishi-mai|ja|麒麟獅子舞|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kirin Shishi-mai|ja_comment=fixed jawiki redirect|3=麒麟獅子|1=Qilin dance|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11677329}} Dance''' - Town-designated Intangible Folk Cultural Property (designated March 31, 1986)<ref>{{Cite web|title=Shichikama Kirin Shishi-mai|url=https://www.town.shinonsen.hyogo.jp/page/?mode=detail&page_id=8ab359199e3f3c495d46db6ceb3791ec|access-date=2022-12-19|publisher=Shin'onsen Town}}</ref>
* '''Sangu Shrine Sacred Forest''' (山宮神社社叢) - Town-designated Natural Monument (designated March 31, 1986), featuring ancient [[:en:Castanopsis sieboldii|Castanopsis]] and [[:en:Chamaecyparis obtusa|Hinoki cypress]] trees, along with thick growths of {{ill|Camellia japonica|ja|ヤブツバキ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Camellia japonica|ja_comment=fixed jawiki redirect|3=ツバキ|1=Camellia japonica|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q160121}} and [[:en:Cleyera japonica|Sakaki]].<ref>{{Cite web|title=Sacred Forest of Shichikama Sangu Shrine|url=https://www.town.shinonsen.hyogo.jp/page/?mode=detail&page_id=b500773414376fa6bd4970ec294860e2|access-date=2022-12-19|publisher=Shin'onsen Town}}</ref>

== Access ==
* [[:en:West Japan Railway Company|West Japan Railway Company]] (JR West) [[:en:San'in Main Line|San'in Main Line]]: From [[:en:Hamasaka Station|Hamasaka Station]], take the {{ill|Zentan Bus|ja|全但バス|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zentan Bus|1=Zentan Bus|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11389110}} bound for {{ill|Yumura Onsen (Hyōgo)|ja|湯村温泉|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yumura Onsen (Hyōgo)|1=Yumura Onsen (Hyōgo)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11563591}}, and get off at "Shichikama Onsen-guchi" bus stop. 5-minute walk.
* From Osaka or Kobe, take the Zentan Bus {{ill|Yumechiyo-go|ja|夢千代号|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yumechiyo-go|ja_comment=fixed jawiki redirect|3=全但バス|1=Zentan Bus|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11389110}} to "Yumura Onsen," transfer to the bus for Hamasaka, and get off at "Shichikama Onsen-guchi." 5-minute walk.

'''Sangu Shrine''' (山宮神社, *Sangu Jinja*) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located at Shichikama, Shin'onsen, Mikata District, [[:en:Hyōgo Prefecture|Hyōgo Prefecture]]. It was formerly classified as a {{ill|Village shrine|ja|村社|1=Son-sha|WD=Q134917284}}{{efn|Former shrine rank|lt=Son-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Village shrine|ja_comment=fixed jawiki redirect|3=近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}. The main deity enshrined is {{ill|Ōyamatsumi|ja|大山祇命|1=Ōyamatsumi|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamatsumi|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamatsumi|ja_comment=fixed jawiki redirect|3=オオヤマツミ}}, with additional enshrinements of [[:en:Izanagi|Izanagi]], [[:en:Izanami|Izanami]], {{ill|Homuda Wake no Mikoto|ja|誉田別尊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Homuda Wake no Mikoto|ja_comment=fixed jawiki redirect|3=応神天皇|1=Ōjin|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q317997}}, and {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|スサノオノミコト|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=fixed jawiki redirect|3=スサノオ}}.

== Overview ==
The shrine is located atop a small forested hill near the source of the {{ill|Shichikama Onsen|ja|七釜温泉|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shichikama Onsen|1=Shichikama Onsen|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11353724}}. The exact founding date is unknown. Before the modern era, it was known as '''Sangami Gongen''' (山神権現). In [[:en:1868|1868]] (the first year of the [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]]), it was renamed to its current title. In [[:en:1873|1873]] (Meiji 6), it was designated a village shrine and enshrined [[:en:Hachiman|Hachiman]] and the local {{ill|Placenta deity|ja|胞衣神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Placenta deity|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.

The shrine's main hall was rebuilt in [[:en:1444|1444]] (Bun'an 1, [[:en:Muromachi period|Muromachi period]]), but was destroyed in [[:en:1944|1944]] (Shōwa 19) due to a typhoon. The current structure was rebuilt after World War II.

== Festivals and Events ==
* Annual Festival: September 29<ref>{{Cite web|title=Festival Calendar - Sangu Shrine|url=https://www.kirinnomachi-japan-heritage.jp/jp/reisai/1059/|access-date=2022-12-19|publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs}}</ref>

== Cultural Properties ==
* '''Shichikama {{ill|Kirin Shishi-mai|ja|麒麟獅子舞|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kirin Shishi-mai|ja_comment=fixed jawiki redirect|3=麒麟獅子|1=Qilin dance|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11677329}} Dance''' - Town-designated Intangible Folk Cultural Property (designated March 31, 1986)<ref>{{Cite web|title=Shichikama Kirin Shishi-mai|url=https://www.town.shinonsen.hyogo.jp/page/?mode=detail&page_id=8ab359199e3f3c495d46db6ceb3791ec|access-date=2022-12-19|publisher=Shin'onsen Town}}</ref>
* '''Sangu Shrine Sacred Forest''' (山宮神社社叢) - Town-designated Natural Monument (designated March 31, 1986), featuring ancient [[:en:Castanopsis sieboldii|Castanopsis]] and [[:en:Chamaecyparis obtusa|Hinoki cypress]] trees, along with thick growths of {{ill|Camellia japonica|ja|ヤブツバキ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Camellia japonica|ja_comment=fixed jawiki redirect|3=ツバキ|1=Camellia japonica|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q160121}} and [[:en:Cleyera japonica|Sakaki]].<ref>{{Cite web|title=Sacred Forest of Shichikama Sangu Shrine|url=https://www.town.shinonsen.hyogo.jp/page/?mode=detail&page_id=b500773414376fa6bd4970ec294860e2|access-date=2022-12-19|publisher=Shin'onsen Town}}</ref>

== Access ==
* [[:en:West Japan Railway Company|West Japan Railway Company]] (JR West) [[:en:San'in Main Line|San'in Main Line]]: From [[:en:Hamasaka Station|Hamasaka Station]], take the {{ill|Zentan Bus|ja|全但バス|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zentan Bus|1=Zentan Bus|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11389110}} bound for {{ill|Yumura Onsen (Hyōgo)|ja|湯村温泉|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yumura Onsen (Hyōgo)|1=Yumura Onsen (Hyōgo)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11563591}}, and get off at "Shichikama Onsen-guchi" bus stop. 5-minute walk.
* From Osaka or Kobe, take the Zentan Bus {{ill|Yumechiyo-go|ja|夢千代号|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yumechiyo-go|ja_comment=fixed jawiki redirect|3=全但バス|1=Zentan Bus|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11389110}} to "Yumura Onsen," transfer to the bus for Hamasaka, and get off at "Shichikama Onsen-guchi." 5-minute walk.

== Nearby Attractions ==

** {{ill|Hamasaka Onsen|ja|浜坂温泉|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hamasaka Onsen|1=Hamasaka Onsen|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11557617}} (9-minute bus ride)

* {{ill|Hamasaka Prefectural Sun Beach|ja|浜坂県民サンビーチ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hamasaka Prefectural Sun Beach|1=Hamasaka Kenmin Sun　Beach|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11557620}}

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:山宮神社]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:山宮神社]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:山宮神社]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11467748]] Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 05:33, 12 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:25, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | Name = Sangu Shrine | image = [[File:Sangu-jinja Shinonsen03s5s4592.jpg|300px]] | location = [[:en:Hyōgo Prefecture|Hyōgo Prefecture]], [[:en:Mikata District, Hyōgo|Mikata District, Hyōgo]], [[:en:Shin'onsen, Hyōgo|Shin'onsen]], Shichikama 1409 | coordinates = {{coord|35|36|39.6|N|134|28|44.7|E|region:JP-28_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | deity = {{ill|Ōyamatsumi|ja|大山祇命|1=Ōyamatsumi|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamatsumi|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamatsumi|ja_comment=fixed jawiki redirect|3=オオヤマツミ}} | Type = Village Shrine (Former) | Established = Unknown | Main Hall = | Alternate Name = | Annual Festival = September 29 | Rituals = | Map = {{location map|Japan Hyōgo|lat_deg=35|lat_min=36|lat_sec=39.6|lon_deg=134|lon_min=28|lon_sec=44.7|label=Sangu Shrine|position=center|width=200|float=center}} }}
'''Sangu Shrine''' (山宮神社, *Sangu Jinja*) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located at Shichikama, Shin'onsen, Mikata District, [[:en:Hyōgo Prefecture|Hyōgo Prefecture]]. It was formerly classified as a {{ill|Village shrine|ja|村社|1=Son-sha|WD=Q134917284}}{{efn|Former shrine rank|lt=Son-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Village shrine|ja_comment=fixed jawiki redirect|3=近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}. The main deity enshrined is {{ill|Ōyamatsumi|ja|大山祇命|1=Ōyamatsumi|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamatsumi|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamatsumi|ja_comment=fixed jawiki redirect|3=オオヤマツミ}}, with additional enshrinements of [[:en:Izanagi|Izanagi]], [[:en:Izanami|Izanami]], {{ill|Homuda Wake no Mikoto|ja|誉田別尊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Homuda Wake no Mikoto|ja_comment=fixed jawiki redirect|3=応神天皇|1=Ōjin|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q317997}}, and {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|スサノオノミコト|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=fixed jawiki redirect|3=スサノオ}}.
== Overview == The shrine is located atop a small forested hill near the source of the {{ill|Shichikama Onsen|ja|七釜温泉|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shichikama Onsen|1=Shichikama Onsen|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11353724}}. The exact founding date is unknown. Before the modern era, it was known as '''Sangami Gongen''' (山神権現). In [[:en:1868|1868]] (the first year of the [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]]), it was renamed to its current title. In [[:en:1873|1873]] (Meiji 6), it was designated a village shrine and enshrined [[:en:Hachiman|Hachiman]] and the local {{ill|Placenta deity|ja|胞衣神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Placenta deity|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.
The shrine's main hall was rebuilt in [[:en:1444|1444]] (Bun'an 1, [[:en:Muromachi period|Muromachi period]]), but was destroyed in [[:en:1944|1944]] (Shōwa 19) due to a typhoon. The current structure was rebuilt after World War II.
== Festivals and Events == * Annual Festival: September 29<ref>{{Cite web|title=Festival Calendar - Sangu Shrine|url=https://www.kirinnomachi-japan-heritage.jp/jp/reisai/1059/|access-date=2022-12-19|publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs}}</ref>
== Cultural Properties == * '''Shichikama {{ill|Kirin Shishi-mai|ja|麒麟獅子舞|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kirin Shishi-mai|ja_comment=fixed jawiki redirect|3=麒麟獅子|1=Qilin dance|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11677329}} Dance''' - Town-designated Intangible Folk Cultural Property (designated March 31, 1986)<ref>{{Cite web|title=Shichikama Kirin Shishi-mai|url=https://www.town.shinonsen.hyogo.jp/page/?mode=detail&page_id=8ab359199e3f3c495d46db6ceb3791ec|access-date=2022-12-19|publisher=Shin'onsen Town}}</ref> * '''Sangu Shrine Sacred Forest''' (山宮神社社叢) - Town-designated Natural Monument (designated March 31, 1986), featuring ancient [[:en:Castanopsis sieboldii|Castanopsis]] and [[:en:Chamaecyparis obtusa|Hinoki cypress]] trees, along with thick growths of {{ill|Camellia japonica|ja|ヤブツバキ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Camellia japonica|ja_comment=fixed jawiki redirect|3=ツバキ|1=Camellia japonica|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q160121}} and [[:en:Cleyera japonica|Sakaki]].<ref>{{Cite web|title=Sacred Forest of Shichikama Sangu Shrine|url=https://www.town.shinonsen.hyogo.jp/page/?mode=detail&page_id=b500773414376fa6bd4970ec294860e2|access-date=2022-12-19|publisher=Shin'onsen Town}}</ref>
== Access == * [[:en:West Japan Railway Company|West Japan Railway Company]] (JR West) [[:en:San'in Main Line|San'in Main Line]]: From [[:en:Hamasaka Station|Hamasaka Station]], take the {{ill|Zentan Bus|ja|全但バス|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zentan Bus|1=Zentan Bus|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11389110}} bound for {{ill|Yumura Onsen (Hyōgo)|ja|湯村温泉|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yumura Onsen (Hyōgo)|1=Yumura Onsen (Hyōgo)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11563591}}, and get off at "Shichikama Onsen-guchi" bus stop. 5-minute walk. * From Osaka or Kobe, take the Zentan Bus {{ill|Yumechiyo-go|ja|夢千代号|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yumechiyo-go|ja_comment=fixed jawiki redirect|3=全但バス|1=Zentan Bus|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11389110}} to "Yumura Onsen," transfer to the bus for Hamasaka, and get off at "Shichikama Onsen-guchi." 5-minute walk.
== Nearby Attractions == * {{ill|Hamasaka Onsenkyō|ja|浜坂温泉郷|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hamasaka Onsenkyō|1=Hamasaka Onsenkyō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11557619}} ** {{ill|Hamasaka Onsen|ja|浜坂温泉|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hamasaka Onsen|1=Hamasaka Onsen|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11557617}} (9-minute bus ride) * {{ill|Yumura Onsen (Hyōgo)|ja|湯村温泉|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yumura Onsen (Hyōgo)|1=Yumura Onsen (Hyōgo)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11563591}} (15-minute bus ride) * {{ill|Hamasaka Prefectural Sun Beach|ja|浜坂県民サンビーチ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hamasaka Prefectural Sun Beach|1=Hamasaka Kenmin Sun Beach|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11557620}} * {{ill|Tajima Mihonoura|ja|但馬御火浦|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tajima Mihonoura|1=Tajima Mihonoura|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11381630}}

The shrine's main hall was rebuilt in [[:en:1444|1444]] (Bun'an 1, [[:en:Muromachi period|Muromachi period]]), but was destroyed in [[:en:1944|1944]] (Shōwa 19) due to a typhoon. The current structure was rebuilt after World War II.
== Festivals and Events == * Annual Festival: September 29<ref>{{Cite web|title=Festival Calendar - Sangu Shrine|url=https://www.kirinnomachi-japan-heritage.jp/jp/reisai/1059/|access-date=2022-12-19|publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs}}</ref>
== Cultural Properties == * '''Shichikama {{ill|Kirin Shishi-mai|ja|麒麟獅子舞|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kirin Shishi-mai|ja_comment=fixed jawiki redirect|3=麒麟獅子|1=Qilin dance|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11677329}} Dance''' - Town-designated Intangible Folk Cultural Property (designated March 31, 1986)<ref>{{Cite web|title=Shichikama Kirin Shishi-mai|url=https://www.town.shinonsen.hyogo.jp/page/?mode=detail&page_id=8ab359199e3f3c495d46db6ceb3791ec|access-date=2022-12-19|publisher=Shin'onsen Town}}</ref> * '''Sangu Shrine Sacred Forest''' (山宮神社社叢) - Town-designated Natural Monument (designated March 31, 1986), featuring ancient [[:en:Castanopsis sieboldii|Castanopsis]] and [[:en:Chamaecyparis obtusa|Hinoki cypress]] trees, along with thick growths of {{ill|Camellia japonica|ja|ヤブツバキ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Camellia japonica|ja_comment=fixed jawiki redirect|3=ツバキ|1=Camellia japonica|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q160121}} and [[:en:Cleyera japonica|Sakaki]].<ref>{{Cite web|title=Sacred Forest of Shichikama Sangu Shrine|url=https://www.town.shinonsen.hyogo.jp/page/?mode=detail&page_id=b500773414376fa6bd4970ec294860e2|access-date=2022-12-19|publisher=Shin'onsen Town}}</ref>
== Access == * [[:en:West Japan Railway Company|West Japan Railway Company]] (JR West) [[:en:San'in Main Line|San'in Main Line]]: From [[:en:Hamasaka Station|Hamasaka Station]], take the {{ill|Zentan Bus|ja|全但バス|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zentan Bus|1=Zentan Bus|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11389110}} bound for {{ill|Yumura Onsen (Hyōgo)|ja|湯村温泉|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yumura Onsen (Hyōgo)|1=Yumura Onsen (Hyōgo)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11563591}}, and get off at "Shichikama Onsen-guchi" bus stop. 5-minute walk. * From Osaka or Kobe, take the Zentan Bus {{ill|Yumechiyo-go|ja|夢千代号|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yumechiyo-go|ja_comment=fixed jawiki redirect|3=全但バス|1=Zentan Bus|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11389110}} to "Yumura Onsen," transfer to the bus for Hamasaka, and get off at "Shichikama Onsen-guchi." 5-minute walk.
== Nearby Attractions == * {{ill|Hamasaka Onsenkyō|ja|浜坂温泉郷|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hamasaka Onsenkyō|1=Hamasaka Onsenkyō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11557619}} ** {{ill|Hamasaka Onsen|ja|浜坂温泉|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hamasaka Onsen|1=Hamasaka Onsen|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11557617}} (9-minute bus ride) * {{ill|Yumura Onsen (Hyōgo)|ja|湯村温泉|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yumura Onsen (Hyōgo)|1=Yumura Onsen (Hyōgo)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11563591}} (15-minute bus ride) * {{ill|Hamasaka Prefectural Sun Beach|ja|浜坂県民サンビーチ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hamasaka Prefectural Sun Beach|1=Hamasaka Kenmin Sun Beach|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11557620}} * {{ill|Tajima Mihonoura|ja|但馬御火浦|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tajima Mihonoura|1=Tajima Mihonoura|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11381630}}

== Related Pages == * {{ill|Sangu Shrine|ja|山宮神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sangu Shrine|1=Yamamiya Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11467748}}
