<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:山宮神社]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:山宮神社]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:山宮神社]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11467748]] Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 05:33, 12 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:25, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | Name = Sangu Shrine | image = [[File:Sangu-jinja Shinonsen03s5s4592.jpg|300px]] | location = {{ill|Hyōgo Prefecture|en|Hyōgo Prefecture|WD=Q130290|lt=Hyōgo Prefecture}}, {{ill|Mikata District, Hyōgo|en|Mikata District, Hyōgo|WD=Q1155035|lt=Mikata District, Hyōgo}}, {{ill|Shin'onsen, Hyōgo|en|Shin'onsen, Hyōgo|WD=Q1154955|lt=Shin'onsen}}, Shichikama 1409 | coordinates = {{coord|35|36|39.6|N|134|28|44.7|E|region:JP-28_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | deity = {{ill|Ōyamatsumi|ja|大山祇命|1=Ōyamatsumi|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamatsumi|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamatsumi|ja_comment=fixed jawiki redirect|3=オオヤマツミ}} | Type = Village Shrine (Former) | Established = Unknown | Main Hall = | Alternate Name = | Annual Festival = September 29 | Rituals = | Map = {{location map|Japan Hyōgo|lat_deg=35|lat_min=36|lat_sec=39.6|lon_deg=134|lon_min=28|lon_sec=44.7|label=Sangu Shrine|position=center|width=200|float=center}} }}
'''Sangu Shrine''' (山宮神社, *Sangu Jinja*) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine}} located at Shichikama, Shin'onsen, Mikata District, {{ill|Hyōgo Prefecture|en|Hyōgo Prefecture|WD=Q130290|lt=Hyōgo Prefecture}}. It was formerly classified as a {{ill|Village shrine|ja|村社|1=Son-sha|WD=Q134917284}}{{efn|Former shrine rank|lt=Son-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Village shrine|ja_comment=fixed jawiki redirect|3=近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}. The main deity enshrined is {{ill|Ōyamatsumi|ja|大山祇命|1=Ōyamatsumi|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamatsumi|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamatsumi|ja_comment=fixed jawiki redirect|3=オオヤマツミ}}, with additional enshrinements of {{ill|Izanagi|en|Izanagi|WD=Q813858|lt=Izanagi}}, {{ill|Izanami|en|Izanami|WD=Q682306|lt=Izanami}}, {{ill|Homuda Wake no Mikoto|ja|誉田別尊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Homuda Wake no Mikoto|ja_comment=fixed jawiki redirect|3=応神天皇|1=Ōjin|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q317997}}, and {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|スサノオノミコト|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=fixed jawiki redirect|3=スサノオ}}.
== Overview == The shrine is located atop a small forested hill near the source of the {{ill|Shichikama Onsen|ja|七釜温泉|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shichikama Onsen|1=Shichikama Onsen|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11353724}}. The exact founding date is unknown. Before the modern era, it was known as '''Sangami Gongen''' (山神権現). In {{ill|1868|en|1868|WD=Q7717|lt=1868}} (the first year of the {{ill|Meiji era|en|Meiji era|WD=Q3125096|lt=Meiji era}}), it was renamed to its current title. In {{ill|1873|en|1873|WD=Q7757|lt=1873}} (Meiji 6), it was designated a village shrine and enshrined {{ill|Hachiman|en|Hachiman|WD=Q261637|lt=Hachiman}} and the local {{ill|Placenta deity|ja|胞衣神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Placenta deity|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.
The shrine's main hall was rebuilt in {{ill|1444|en|1444|WD=Q6420|lt=1444}} (Bun'an 1, {{ill|Muromachi period|en|Muromachi period|WD=Q334845|lt=Muromachi period}}), but was destroyed in {{ill|1944|en|1944|WD=Q5268|lt=1944}} (Shōwa 19) due to a typhoon. The current structure was rebuilt after World War II.
== Festivals and Events == * Annual Festival: September 29<ref>{{Cite web|title=Festival Calendar - Sangu Shrine|url=https://www.kirinnomachi-japan-heritage.jp/jp/reisai/1059/|access-date=2022-12-19|publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs}}</ref>
== Cultural Properties == * '''Shichikama {{ill|Kirin Shishi-mai|ja|麒麟獅子舞|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kirin Shishi-mai|ja_comment=fixed jawiki redirect|3=麒麟獅子|1=Qilin dance|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11677329}} Dance''' - Town-designated Intangible Folk Cultural Property (designated March 31, 1986)<ref>{{Cite web|title=Shichikama Kirin Shishi-mai|url=https://www.town.shinonsen.hyogo.jp/page/?mode=detail&page_id=8ab359199e3f3c495d46db6ceb3791ec|access-date=2022-12-19|publisher=Shin'onsen Town}}</ref> * '''Sangu Shrine Sacred Forest''' (山宮神社社叢) - Town-designated Natural Monument (designated March 31, 1986), featuring ancient {{ill|Castanopsis sieboldii|en|Castanopsis sieboldii|WD=Q846994|lt=Castanopsis}} and {{ill|Chamaecyparis obtusa|en|Chamaecyparis obtusa|WD=Q917187|lt=Hinoki cypress}} trees, along with thick growths of {{ill|Camellia japonica|ja|ヤブツバキ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Camellia japonica|ja_comment=fixed jawiki redirect|3=ツバキ|1=Camellia japonica|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q160121}} and {{ill|Cleyera japonica|en|Cleyera japonica|WD=Q775470|lt=Sakaki}}.<ref>{{Cite web|title=Sacred Forest of Shichikama Sangu Shrine|url=https://www.town.shinonsen.hyogo.jp/page/?mode=detail&page_id=b500773414376fa6bd4970ec294860e2|access-date=2022-12-19|publisher=Shin'onsen Town}}</ref>
== Access == * {{ill|West Japan Railway Company|en|West Japan Railway Company|WD=Q502125|lt=West Japan Railway Company}} (JR West) {{ill|San'in Main Line|en|San'in Main Line|WD=Q1135170|lt=San'in Main Line}}: From {{ill|Hamasaka Station|en|Hamasaka Station|WD=Q872032|lt=Hamasaka Station}}, take the {{ill|Zentan Bus|ja|全但バス|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zentan Bus|1=Zentan Bus|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11389110}} bound for {{ill|Yumura Onsen (Hyōgo)|ja|湯村温泉|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yumura Onsen (Hyōgo)|1=Yumura Onsen (Hyōgo)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11563591}}, and get off at "Shichikama Onsen-guchi" bus stop. 5-minute walk. * From Osaka or Kobe, take the Zentan Bus {{ill|Yumechiyo-go|ja|夢千代号|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yumechiyo-go|ja_comment=fixed jawiki redirect|3=全但バス|1=Zentan Bus|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11389110}} to "Yumura Onsen," transfer to the bus for Hamasaka, and get off at "Shichikama Onsen-guchi." 5-minute walk.
== Nearby Attractions == * {{ill|Hamasaka Onsenkyō|ja|浜坂温泉郷|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hamasaka Onsenkyō|1=Hamasaka Onsenkyō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11557619}} ** {{ill|Hamasaka Onsen|ja|浜坂温泉|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hamasaka Onsen|1=Hamasaka Onsen|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11557617}} (9-minute bus ride) * {{ill|Yumura Onsen (Hyōgo)|ja|湯村温泉|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yumura Onsen (Hyōgo)|1=Yumura Onsen (Hyōgo)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11563591}} (15-minute bus ride) * {{ill|Hamasaka Prefectural Sun Beach|ja|浜坂県民サンビーチ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hamasaka Prefectural Sun Beach|1=Hamasaka Kenmin Sun Beach|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11557620}} * {{ill|Tajima Mihonoura|ja|但馬御火浦|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tajima Mihonoura|1=Tajima Mihonoura|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11381630}}

In {{ill|1868|en|1868|WD=Q7717|lt=1868}} (the first year of the {{ill|Meiji era|en|Meiji era|WD=Q3125096|lt=Meiji era}}), it was renamed to its current title. <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:山宮神社]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:山宮神社]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:山宮神社]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11467748]] Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 05:33, 12 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:25, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | Name = Sangu Shrine | image = [[File:Sangu-jinja Shinonsen03s5s4592.jpg|300px]] | location = {{ill|Hyōgo Prefecture|en|Hyōgo Prefecture|WD=Q130290|lt=Hyōgo Prefecture}}, {{ill|Mikata District, Hyōgo|en|Mikata District, Hyōgo|WD=Q1155035|lt=Mikata District, Hyōgo}}, {{ill|Shin'onsen, Hyōgo|en|Shin'onsen, Hyōgo|WD=Q1154955|lt=Shin'onsen}}, Shichikama 1409 | coordinates = {{coord|35|36|39.6|N|134|28|44.7|E|region:JP-28_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | deity = {{ill|Ōyamatsumi|ja|大山祇命|1=Ōyamatsumi|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamatsumi|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamatsumi|ja_comment=fixed jawiki redirect|3=オオヤマツミ}} | Type = Village Shrine (Former) | Established = Unknown | Main Hall = | Alternate Name = | Annual Festival = September 29 | Rituals = | Map = {{location map|Japan Hyōgo|lat_deg=35|lat_min=36|lat_sec=39.6|lon_deg=134|lon_min=28|lon_sec=44.7|label=Sangu Shrine|position=center|width=200|float=center}} }}
'''Sangu Shrine''' (山宮神社, *Sangu Jinja*) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine}} located at Shichikama, Shin'onsen, Mikata District, {{ill|Hyōgo Prefecture|en|Hyōgo Prefecture|WD=Q130290|lt=Hyōgo Prefecture}}. It was formerly classified as a {{ill|Village shrine|ja|村社|1=Son-sha|WD=Q134917284}}{{efn|Former shrine rank|lt=Son-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Village shrine|ja_comment=fixed jawiki redirect|3=近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}. The main deity enshrined is {{ill|Ōyamatsumi|ja|大山祇命|1=Ōyamatsumi|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamatsumi|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamatsumi|ja_comment=fixed jawiki redirect|3=オオヤマツミ}}, with additional enshrinements of {{ill|Izanagi|en|Izanagi|WD=Q813858|lt=Izanagi}}, {{ill|Izanami|en|Izanami|WD=Q682306|lt=Izanami}}, {{ill|Homuda Wake no Mikoto|ja|誉田別尊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Homuda Wake no Mikoto|ja_comment=fixed jawiki redirect|3=応神天皇|1=Ōjin|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q317997}}, and {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|スサノオノミコト|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=fixed jawiki redirect|3=スサノオ}}.
== Overview == The shrine is located atop a small forested hill near the source of the {{ill|Shichikama Onsen|ja|七釜温泉|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shichikama Onsen|1=Shichikama Onsen|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11353724}}. The exact founding date is unknown. Before the modern era, it was known as '''Sangami Gongen''' (山神権現). In {{ill|1868|en|1868|WD=Q7717|lt=1868}} (the first year of the {{ill|Meiji era|en|Meiji era|WD=Q3125096|lt=Meiji era}}), it was renamed to its current title. In {{ill|1873|en|1873|WD=Q7757|lt=1873}} (Meiji 6), it was designated a village shrine and enshrined {{ill|Hachiman|en|Hachiman|WD=Q261637|lt=Hachiman}} and the local {{ill|Placenta deity|ja|胞衣神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Placenta deity|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.
The shrine's main hall was rebuilt in {{ill|1444|en|1444|WD=Q6420|lt=1444}} (Bun'an 1, {{ill|Muromachi period|en|Muromachi period|WD=Q334845|lt=Muromachi period}}), but was destroyed in {{ill|1944|en|1944|WD=Q5268|lt=1944}} (Shōwa 19) due to a typhoon. The current structure was rebuilt after World War II.
== Festivals and Events == * Annual Festival: September 29<ref>{{Cite web|title=Festival Calendar - Sangu Shrine|url=https://www.kirinnomachi-japan-heritage.jp/jp/reisai/1059/|access-date=2022-12-19|publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs}}</ref>
== Cultural Properties == * '''Shichikama {{ill|Kirin Shishi-mai|ja|麒麟獅子舞|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kirin Shishi-mai|ja_comment=fixed jawiki redirect|3=麒麟獅子|1=Qilin dance|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11677329}} Dance''' - Town-designated Intangible Folk Cultural Property (designated March 31, 1986)<ref>{{Cite web|title=Shichikama Kirin Shishi-mai|url=https://www.town.shinonsen.hyogo.jp/page/?mode=detail&page_id=8ab359199e3f3c495d46db6ceb3791ec|access-date=2022-12-19|publisher=Shin'onsen Town}}</ref> * '''Sangu Shrine Sacred Forest''' (山宮神社社叢) - Town-designated Natural Monument (designated March 31, 1986), featuring ancient {{ill|Castanopsis sieboldii|en|Castanopsis sieboldii|WD=Q846994|lt=Castanopsis}} and {{ill|Chamaecyparis obtusa|en|Chamaecyparis obtusa|WD=Q917187|lt=Hinoki cypress}} trees, along with thick growths of {{ill|Camellia japonica|ja|ヤブツバキ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Camellia japonica|ja_comment=fixed jawiki redirect|3=ツバキ|1=Camellia japonica|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q160121}} and {{ill|Cleyera japonica|en|Cleyera japonica|WD=Q775470|lt=Sakaki}}.<ref>{{Cite web|title=Sacred Forest of Shichikama Sangu Shrine|url=https://www.town.shinonsen.hyogo.jp/page/?mode=detail&page_id=b500773414376fa6bd4970ec294860e2|access-date=2022-12-19|publisher=Shin'onsen Town}}</ref>
== Access == * {{ill|West Japan Railway Company|en|West Japan Railway Company|WD=Q502125|lt=West Japan Railway Company}} (JR West) {{ill|San'in Main Line|en|San'in Main Line|WD=Q1135170|lt=San'in Main Line}}: From {{ill|Hamasaka Station|en|Hamasaka Station|WD=Q872032|lt=Hamasaka Station}}, take the {{ill|Zentan Bus|ja|全但バス|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zentan Bus|1=Zentan Bus|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11389110}} bound for {{ill|Yumura Onsen (Hyōgo)|ja|湯村温泉|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yumura Onsen (Hyōgo)|1=Yumura Onsen (Hyōgo)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11563591}}, and get off at "Shichikama Onsen-guchi" bus stop. 5-minute walk. * From Osaka or Kobe, take the Zentan Bus {{ill|Yumechiyo-go|ja|夢千代号|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yumechiyo-go|ja_comment=fixed jawiki redirect|3=全但バス|1=Zentan Bus|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11389110}} to "Yumura Onsen," transfer to the bus for Hamasaka, and get off at "Shichikama Onsen-guchi." 5-minute walk.
== Nearby Attractions == * {{ill|Hamasaka Onsenkyō|ja|浜坂温泉郷|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hamasaka Onsenkyō|1=Hamasaka Onsenkyō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11557619}} ** {{ill|Hamasaka Onsen|ja|浜坂温泉|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hamasaka Onsen|1=Hamasaka Onsen|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11557617}} (9-minute bus ride) * {{ill|Yumura Onsen (Hyōgo)|ja|湯村温泉|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yumura Onsen (Hyōgo)|1=Yumura Onsen (Hyōgo)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11563591}} (15-minute bus ride) * {{ill|Hamasaka Prefectural Sun Beach|ja|浜坂県民サンビーチ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hamasaka Prefectural Sun Beach|1=Hamasaka Kenmin Sun Beach|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11557620}} * {{ill|Tajima Mihonoura|ja|但馬御火浦|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tajima Mihonoura|1=Tajima Mihonoura|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11381630}}

The current structure was rebuilt after World War II.

== Cultural Properties == * '''Shichikama {{ill|Kirin Shishi-mai|ja|麒麟獅子舞|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kirin Shishi-mai|ja_comment=fixed jawiki redirect|3=麒麟獅子|1=Qilin dance|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11677329}} Dance''' - Town-designated Intangible Folk Cultural Property (designated March 31, 1986)<ref>{{Cite web|title=Shichikama Kirin Shishi-mai|url=https://www.town.shinonsen.hyogo.jp/page/?mode=detail&page_id=8ab359199e3f3c495d46db6ceb3791ec|access-date=2022-12-19|publisher=Shin'onsen Town}}</ref> * '''Sangu Shrine Sacred Forest''' (山宮神社社叢) - Town-designated Natural Monument (designated March 31, 1986), featuring ancient {{ill|Castanopsis sieboldii|en|Castanopsis sieboldii|WD=Q846994|lt=Castanopsis}} and {{ill|Chamaecyparis obtusa|en|Chamaecyparis obtusa|WD=Q917187|lt=Hinoki cypress}} trees, along with thick growths of {{ill|Camellia japonica|ja|ヤブツバキ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Camellia japonica|ja_comment=fixed jawiki redirect|3=ツバキ|1=Camellia japonica|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q160121}} and {{ill|Cleyera japonica|en|Cleyera japonica|WD=Q775470|lt=Sakaki}}.<ref>{{Cite web|title=Sacred Forest of Shichikama Sangu Shrine|url=https://www.town.shinonsen.hyogo.jp/page/?mode=detail&page_id=b500773414376fa6bd4970ec294860e2|access-date=2022-12-19|publisher=Shin'onsen Town}}</ref>
== Access == * {{ill|West Japan Railway Company|en|West Japan Railway Company|WD=Q502125|lt=West Japan Railway Company}} (JR West) {{ill|San'in Main Line|en|San'in Main Line|WD=Q1135170|lt=San'in Main Line}}: From {{ill|Hamasaka Station|en|Hamasaka Station|WD=Q872032|lt=Hamasaka Station}}, take the {{ill|Zentan Bus|ja|全但バス|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zentan Bus|1=Zentan Bus|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11389110}} bound for {{ill|Yumura Onsen (Hyōgo)|ja|湯村温泉|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yumura Onsen (Hyōgo)|1=Yumura Onsen (Hyōgo)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11563591}}, and get off at "Shichikama Onsen-guchi" bus stop. 5-minute walk. * From Osaka or Kobe, take the Zentan Bus {{ill|Yumechiyo-go|ja|夢千代号|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yumechiyo-go|ja_comment=fixed jawiki redirect|3=全但バス|1=Zentan Bus|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11389110}} to "Yumura Onsen," transfer to the bus for Hamasaka, and get off at "Shichikama Onsen-guchi." 5-minute walk.
== Nearby Attractions == * {{ill|Hamasaka Onsenkyō|ja|浜坂温泉郷|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hamasaka Onsenkyō|1=Hamasaka Onsenkyō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11557619}} ** {{ill|Hamasaka Onsen|ja|浜坂温泉|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hamasaka Onsen|1=Hamasaka Onsen|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11557617}} (9-minute bus ride) * {{ill|Yumura Onsen (Hyōgo)|ja|湯村温泉|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yumura Onsen (Hyōgo)|1=Yumura Onsen (Hyōgo)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11563591}} (15-minute bus ride) * {{ill|Hamasaka Prefectural Sun Beach|ja|浜坂県民サンビーチ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hamasaka Prefectural Sun Beach|1=Hamasaka Kenmin Sun Beach|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11557620}} * {{ill|Tajima Mihonoura|ja|但馬御火浦|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tajima Mihonoura|1=Tajima Mihonoura|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11381630}}

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:山宮神社]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:山宮神社]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:山宮神社]]
{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | Name = Sangu Shrine | image = [[File:Sangu-jinja Shinonsen03s5s4592.jpg|300px]] | location = {{ill|Hyōgo Prefecture|en|Hyōgo Prefecture|WD=Q130290|lt=Hyōgo Prefecture}}, {{ill|Mikata District, Hyōgo|en|Mikata District, Hyōgo|WD=Q1155035|lt=Mikata District, Hyōgo}}, {{ill|Shin'onsen, Hyōgo|en|Shin'onsen, Hyōgo|WD=Q1154955|lt=Shin'onsen}}, Shichikama 1409 | coordinates = {{coord|35|36|39.6|N|134|28|44.7|E|region:JP-28_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | deity = {{ill|Ōyamatsumi|ja|大山祇命|1=Ōyamatsumi|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamatsumi|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamatsumi|ja_comment=fixed jawiki redirect|3=オオヤマツミ}} | Type = Village Shrine (Former) | Established = Unknown | Main Hall = | Alternate Name = | Annual Festival = September 29 | Rituals = | Map = {{location map|Japan Hyōgo|lat_deg=35|lat_min=36|lat_sec=39.6|lon_deg=134|lon_min=28|lon_sec=44.7|label=Sangu Shrine|position=center|width=200|float=center}} }}
'''Sangu Shrine''' (山宮神社, *Sangu Jinja*) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine}} located at Shichikama, Shin'onsen, Mikata District, {{ill|Hyōgo Prefecture|en|Hyōgo Prefecture|WD=Q130290|lt=Hyōgo Prefecture}}. It was formerly classified as a {{ill|Village shrine|ja|村社|1=Son-sha|WD=Q134917284}}{{efn|Former shrine rank|lt=Son-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Village shrine|ja_comment=fixed jawiki redirect|3=近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}. The main deity enshrined is {{ill|Ōyamatsumi|ja|大山祇命|1=Ōyamatsumi|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamatsumi|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamatsumi|ja_comment=fixed jawiki redirect|3=オオヤマツミ}}, with additional enshrinements of {{ill|Izanagi|en|Izanagi|WD=Q813858|lt=Izanagi}}, {{ill|Izanami|en|Izanami|WD=Q682306|lt=Izanami}}, {{ill|Homuda Wake no Mikoto|ja|誉田別尊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Homuda Wake no Mikoto|ja_comment=fixed jawiki redirect|3=応神天皇|1=Ōjin|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q317997}}, and {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|スサノオノミコト|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=fixed jawiki redirect|3=スサノオ}}.
== Overview == The shrine is located atop a small forested hill near the source of the {{ill|Shichikama Onsen|ja|七釜温泉|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shichikama Onsen|1=Shichikama Onsen|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11353724}}. The exact founding date is unknown. Before the modern era, it was known as '''Sangami Gongen''' (山神権現). In {{ill|1868|en|1868|WD=Q7717|lt=1868}} (the first year of the {{ill|Meiji era|en|Meiji era|WD=Q3125096|lt=Meiji era}}), it was renamed to its current title. In {{ill|1873|en|1873|WD=Q7757|lt=1873}} (Meiji 6), it was designated a village shrine and enshrined {{ill|Hachiman|en|Hachiman|WD=Q261637|lt=Hachiman}} and the local {{ill|Placenta deity|ja|胞衣神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Placenta deity|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.
The shrine's main hall was rebuilt in {{ill|1444|en|1444|WD=Q6420|lt=1444}} (Bun'an 1, {{ill|Muromachi period|en|Muromachi period|WD=Q334845|lt=Muromachi period}}), but was destroyed in {{ill|1944|en|1944|WD=Q5268|lt=1944}} (Shōwa 19) due to a typhoon. The current structure was rebuilt after World War II.
== Festivals and Events == * Annual Festival: September 29<ref>{{Cite web|title=Festival Calendar - Sangu Shrine|url=https://www.kirinnomachi-japan-heritage.jp/jp/reisai/1059/|access-date=2022-12-19|publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs}}</ref>
== Cultural Properties == * '''Shichikama {{ill|Kirin Shishi-mai|ja|麒麟獅子舞|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kirin Shishi-mai|ja_comment=fixed jawiki redirect|3=麒麟獅子|1=Qilin dance|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11677329}} Dance''' - Town-designated Intangible Folk Cultural Property (designated March 31, 1986)<ref>{{Cite web|title=Shichikama Kirin Shishi-mai|url=https://www.town.shinonsen.hyogo.jp/page/?mode=detail&page_id=8ab359199e3f3c495d46db6ceb3791ec|access-date=2022-12-19|publisher=Shin'onsen Town}}</ref> * '''Sangu Shrine Sacred Forest''' (山宮神社社叢) - Town-designated Natural Monument (designated March 31, 1986), featuring ancient {{ill|Castanopsis sieboldii|en|Castanopsis sieboldii|WD=Q846994|lt=Castanopsis}} and {{ill|Chamaecyparis obtusa|en|Chamaecyparis obtusa|WD=Q917187|lt=Hinoki cypress}} trees, along with thick growths of {{ill|Camellia japonica|ja|ヤブツバキ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Camellia japonica|ja_comment=fixed jawiki redirect|3=ツバキ|1=Camellia japonica|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q160121}} and {{ill|Cleyera japonica|en|Cleyera japonica|WD=Q775470|lt=Sakaki}}.<ref>{{Cite web|title=Sacred Forest of Shichikama Sangu Shrine|url=https://www.town.shinonsen.hyogo.jp/page/?mode=detail&page_id=b500773414376fa6bd4970ec294860e2|access-date=2022-12-19|publisher=Shin'onsen Town}}</ref>
== Access == * {{ill|West Japan Railway Company|en|West Japan Railway Company|WD=Q502125|lt=West Japan Railway Company}} (JR West) {{ill|San'in Main Line|en|San'in Main Line|WD=Q1135170|lt=San'in Main Line}}: From {{ill|Hamasaka Station|en|Hamasaka Station|WD=Q872032|lt=Hamasaka Station}}, take the {{ill|Zentan Bus|ja|全但バス|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zentan Bus|1=Zentan Bus|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11389110}} bound for {{ill|Yumura Onsen (Hyōgo)|ja|湯村温泉|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yumura Onsen (Hyōgo)|1=Yumura Onsen (Hyōgo)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11563591}}, and get off at "Shichikama Onsen-guchi" bus stop. 5-minute walk. * From Osaka or Kobe, take the Zentan Bus {{ill|Yumechiyo-go|ja|夢千代号|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yumechiyo-go|ja_comment=fixed jawiki redirect|3=全但バス|1=Zentan Bus|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11389110}} to "Yumura Onsen," transfer to the bus for Hamasaka, and get off at "Shichikama Onsen-guchi." 5-minute walk.
== Nearby Attractions == * {{ill|Hamasaka Onsenkyō|ja|浜坂温泉郷|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hamasaka Onsenkyō|1=Hamasaka Onsenkyō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11557619}} ** {{ill|Hamasaka Onsen|ja|浜坂温泉|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hamasaka Onsen|1=Hamasaka Onsen|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11557617}} (9-minute bus ride) * {{ill|Yumura Onsen (Hyōgo)|ja|湯村温泉|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yumura Onsen (Hyōgo)|1=Yumura Onsen (Hyōgo)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11563591}} (15-minute bus ride) * {{ill|Hamasaka Prefectural Sun Beach|ja|浜坂県民サンビーチ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hamasaka Prefectural Sun Beach|1=Hamasaka Kenmin Sun Beach|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11557620}} * {{ill|Tajima Mihonoura|ja|但馬御火浦|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tajima Mihonoura|1=Tajima Mihonoura|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11381630}}

{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | Name = Sangu Shrine | image = [[File:Sangu-jinja Shinonsen03s5s4592.jpg|300px]] | location = {{ill|Hyōgo Prefecture|en|Hyōgo Prefecture|WD=Q130290|lt=Hyōgo Prefecture|1=Hyōgo Prefecture}}, {{ill|Mikata District, Hyōgo|en|Mikata District, Hyōgo|WD=Q1155035|lt=Mikata District, Hyōgo|1=Mikata District, Hyōgo}}, {{ill|Shin'onsen, Hyōgo|en|Shin'onsen, Hyōgo|WD=Q1154955|lt=Shin'onsen|1=Shin'onsen, Hyōgo}}, Shichikama 1409 | coordinates = {{coord|35|36|39.6|N|134|28|44.7|E|region:JP-28_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | deity = {{ill|Ōyamatsumi|ja|大山祇命|1=Ōyamatsumi|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamatsumi|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamatsumi|ja_comment=fixed jawiki redirect|3=オオヤマツミ}} | Type = Village Shrine (Former) | Established = Unknown | Main Hall = | Alternate Name = | Annual Festival = September 29 | Rituals = | Map = {{location map|Japan Hyōgo|lat_deg=35|lat_min=36|lat_sec=39.6|lon_deg=134|lon_min=28|lon_sec=44.7|label=Sangu Shrine|position=center|width=200|float=center}} }}
'''Sangu Shrine''' (山宮神社, *Sangu Jinja*) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine|1=Shinto shrine}} located at Shichikama, Shin'onsen, Mikata District, {{ill|Hyōgo Prefecture|en|Hyōgo Prefecture|WD=Q130290|lt=Hyōgo Prefecture|1=Hyōgo Prefecture}}. It was formerly classified as a {{ill|Village shrine|ja|村社|1=Son-sha|WD=Q134917284}}{{efn|Former shrine rank|lt=Son-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Village shrine|ja_comment=fixed jawiki redirect|3=近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}. The main deity enshrined is {{ill|Ōyamatsumi|ja|大山祇命|1=Ōyamatsumi|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamatsumi|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamatsumi|ja_comment=fixed jawiki redirect|3=オオヤマツミ}}, with additional enshrinements of {{ill|Izanagi|en|Izanagi|WD=Q813858|lt=Izanagi|1=Izanagi}}, {{ill|Izanami|en|Izanami|WD=Q682306|lt=Izanami|1=Izanami}}, {{ill|Homuda Wake no Mikoto|ja|誉田別尊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Homuda Wake no Mikoto|ja_comment=fixed jawiki redirect|3=応神天皇|1=Emperor Ōjin|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q317997}}, and {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|スサノオノミコト|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=fixed jawiki redirect|3=スサノオ}}.
== Overview == The shrine is located atop a small forested hill near the source of the {{ill|Shichikama Onsen|ja|七釜温泉|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shichikama Onsen|1=Shichikama Onsen|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11353724}}. The exact founding date is unknown. Before the modern era, it was known as '''Sangami Gongen''' (山神権現). In {{ill|1868|en|1868|WD=Q7717|lt=1868|1=1868}} (the first year of the {{ill|Meiji era|en|Meiji era|WD=Q3125096|lt=Meiji era|1=Meiji era}}), it was renamed to its current title. In {{ill|1873|en|1873|WD=Q7757|lt=1873|1=1873}} (Meiji 6), it was designated a village shrine and enshrined {{ill|Hachiman|en|Hachiman|WD=Q261637|lt=Hachiman|1=Hachiman}} and the local {{ill|Placenta deity|ja|胞衣神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Placenta deity|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.
The shrine's main hall was rebuilt in {{ill|1444|en|1444|WD=Q6420|lt=1444|1=1444}} (Bun'an 1, {{ill|Muromachi period|en|Muromachi period|WD=Q334845|lt=Muromachi period|1=Muromachi period}}), but was destroyed in {{ill|1944|en|1944|WD=Q5268|lt=1944|1=1944}} (Shōwa 19) due to a typhoon. The current structure was rebuilt after World War II.
== Festivals and Events == * Annual Festival: September 29<ref>{{Cite web|title=Festival Calendar - Sangu Shrine|url=https://www.kirinnomachi-japan-heritage.jp/jp/reisai/1059/|access-date=2022-12-19|publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs}}</ref>
== Cultural Properties == * '''Shichikama {{ill|Kirin Shishi-mai|ja|麒麟獅子舞|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kirin Shishi-mai|ja_comment=fixed jawiki redirect|3=麒麟獅子|1=Qilin dance|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11677329}} Dance''' - Town-designated Intangible Folk Cultural Property (designated March 31, 1986)<ref>{{Cite web|title=Shichikama Kirin Shishi-mai|url=https://www.town.shinonsen.hyogo.jp/page/?mode=detail&page_id=8ab359199e3f3c495d46db6ceb3791ec|access-date=2022-12-19|publisher=Shin'onsen Town}}</ref> * '''Sangu Shrine Sacred Forest''' (山宮神社社叢) - Town-designated Natural Monument (designated March 31, 1986), featuring ancient {{ill|Castanopsis sieboldii|en|Castanopsis sieboldii|WD=Q846994|lt=Castanopsis|1=Castanopsis sieboldii}} and {{ill|Chamaecyparis obtusa|en|Chamaecyparis obtusa|WD=Q917187|lt=Hinoki cypress|1=Chamaecyparis obtusa}} trees, along with thick growths of {{ill|Camellia japonica|ja|ヤブツバキ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Camellia japonica|ja_comment=fixed jawiki redirect|3=ツバキ|1=Camellia japonica|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q160121}} and {{ill|Cleyera japonica|en|Cleyera japonica|WD=Q775470|lt=Sakaki|1=Cleyera japonica}}.<ref>{{Cite web|title=Sacred Forest of Shichikama Sangu Shrine|url=https://www.town.shinonsen.hyogo.jp/page/?mode=detail&page_id=b500773414376fa6bd4970ec294860e2|access-date=2022-12-19|publisher=Shin'onsen Town}}</ref>
== Access == * {{ill|West Japan Railway Company|en|West Japan Railway Company|WD=Q502125|lt=West Japan Railway Company|1=West Japan Railway Company}} (JR West) {{ill|San'in Main Line|en|San'in Main Line|WD=Q1135170|lt=San'in Main Line|1=San'in Main Line}}: From {{ill|Hamasaka Station|en|Hamasaka Station|WD=Q872032|lt=Hamasaka Station|1=Hamasaka Station}}, take the {{ill|Zentan Bus|ja|全但バス|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zentan Bus|1=Zentan Bus|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11389110}} bound for {{ill|Yumura Onsen (Hyōgo)|ja|湯村温泉|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yumura Onsen (Hyōgo)|1=Q11563591|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11563591}}, and get off at "Shichikama Onsen-guchi" bus stop. 5-minute walk. * From Osaka or Kobe, take the Zentan Bus {{ill|Yumechiyo-go|ja|夢千代号|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yumechiyo-go|ja_comment=fixed jawiki redirect|3=全但バス|1=Zentan Bus|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11389110}} to "Yumura Onsen," transfer to the bus for Hamasaka, and get off at "Shichikama Onsen-guchi." 5-minute walk.
== Nearby Attractions == * {{ill|Hamasaka Onsenkyō|ja|浜坂温泉郷|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hamasaka Onsenkyō|1=Hamasaka Onsenkyō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11557619}} ** {{ill|Hamasaka Onsen|ja|浜坂温泉|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hamasaka Onsen|1=Hamasaka Onsen|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11557617}} (9-minute bus ride) * {{ill|Yumura Onsen (Hyōgo)|ja|湯村温泉|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yumura Onsen (Hyōgo)|1=Q11563591|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11563591}} (15-minute bus ride) * {{ill|Hamasaka Prefectural Sun Beach|ja|浜坂県民サンビーチ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hamasaka Prefectural Sun Beach|1=Hamasaka Kenmin Sun Beach|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11557620}} * {{ill|Tajima Mihonoura|ja|但馬御火浦|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tajima Mihonoura|1=Tajima Mihonoura|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11381630}}

The main deity enshrined is {{ill|Ōyamatsumi|ja|大山祇命|1=Ōyamatsumi|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamatsumi|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamatsumi|ja_comment=fixed jawiki redirect|3=オオヤマツミ}}, with additional enshrinements of {{ill|Izanagi|en|Izanagi|WD=Q813858|lt=Izanagi|1=Izanagi}}, {{ill|Izanami|en|Izanami|WD=Q682306|lt=Izanami|1=Izanami}}, {{ill|Homuda Wake no Mikoto|ja|誉田別尊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Homuda Wake no Mikoto|ja_comment=fixed jawiki redirect|3=応神天皇|1=Emperor Ōjin|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q317997}}, and {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|スサノオノミコト|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=fixed jawiki redirect|3=スサノオ}}.
== Overview == The shrine is located atop a small forested hill near the source of the {{ill|Shichikama Onsen|ja|七釜温泉|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shichikama Onsen|1=Shichikama Onsen|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11353724}}. The exact founding date is unknown. Before the modern era, it was known as '''Sangami Gongen''' (山神権現). In {{ill|1868|en|1868|WD=Q7717|lt=1868|1=1868}} (the first year of the {{ill|Meiji era|en|Meiji era|WD=Q3125096|lt=Meiji era|1=Meiji era}}), it was renamed to its current title. In {{ill|1873|en|1873|WD=Q7757|lt=1873|1=1873}} (Meiji 6), it was designated a village shrine and enshrined {{ill|Hachiman|en|Hachiman|WD=Q261637|lt=Hachiman|1=Hachiman}} and the local {{ill|Placenta deity|ja|胞衣神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Placenta deity|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.

The current structure was rebuilt after World War II.
== Festivals and Events == * Annual Festival: September 29<ref>{{Cite web|title=Festival Calendar - Sangu Shrine|url=https://www.kirinnomachi-japan-heritage.jp/jp/reisai/1059/|access-date=2022-12-19|publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs}}</ref>
== Cultural Properties == * '''Shichikama {{ill|Kirin Shishi-mai|ja|麒麟獅子舞|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kirin Shishi-mai|ja_comment=fixed jawiki redirect|3=麒麟獅子|1=Qilin dance|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11677329}} Dance''' - Town-designated Intangible Folk Cultural Property (designated March 31, 1986)<ref>{{Cite web|title=Shichikama Kirin Shishi-mai|url=https://www.town.shinonsen.hyogo.jp/page/?mode=detail&page_id=8ab359199e3f3c495d46db6ceb3791ec|access-date=2022-12-19|publisher=Shin'onsen Town}}</ref> * '''Sangu Shrine Sacred Forest''' (山宮神社社叢) - Town-designated Natural Monument (designated March 31, 1986), featuring ancient {{ill|Castanopsis sieboldii|en|Castanopsis sieboldii|WD=Q846994|lt=Castanopsis|1=Castanopsis sieboldii}} and {{ill|Chamaecyparis obtusa|en|Chamaecyparis obtusa|WD=Q917187|lt=Hinoki cypress|1=Chamaecyparis obtusa}} trees, along with thick growths of {{ill|Camellia japonica|ja|ヤブツバキ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Camellia japonica|ja_comment=fixed jawiki redirect|3=ツバキ|1=Camellia japonica|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q160121}} and {{ill|Cleyera japonica|en|Cleyera japonica|WD=Q775470|lt=Sakaki|1=Cleyera japonica}}.<ref>{{Cite web|title=Sacred Forest of Shichikama Sangu Shrine|url=https://www.town.shinonsen.hyogo.jp/page/?mode=detail&page_id=b500773414376fa6bd4970ec294860e2|access-date=2022-12-19|publisher=Shin'onsen Town}}</ref>
== Access == * {{ill|West Japan Railway Company|en|West Japan Railway Company|WD=Q502125|lt=West Japan Railway Company|1=West Japan Railway Company}} (JR West) {{ill|San'in Main Line|en|San'in Main Line|WD=Q1135170|lt=San'in Main Line|1=San'in Main Line}}: From {{ill|Hamasaka Station|en|Hamasaka Station|WD=Q872032|lt=Hamasaka Station|1=Hamasaka Station}}, take the {{ill|Zentan Bus|ja|全但バス|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zentan Bus|1=Zentan Bus|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11389110}} bound for {{ill|Yumura Onsen (Hyōgo)|ja|湯村温泉|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yumura Onsen (Hyōgo)|1=Q11563591|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11563591}}, and get off at "Shichikama Onsen-guchi" bus stop. 5-minute walk. * From Osaka or Kobe, take the Zentan Bus {{ill|Yumechiyo-go|ja|夢千代号|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yumechiyo-go|ja_comment=fixed jawiki redirect|3=全但バス|1=Zentan Bus|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11389110}} to "Yumura Onsen," transfer to the bus for Hamasaka, and get off at "Shichikama Onsen-guchi." 5-minute walk.
== Nearby Attractions == * {{ill|Hamasaka Onsenkyō|ja|浜坂温泉郷|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hamasaka Onsenkyō|1=Hamasaka Onsenkyō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11557619}} ** {{ill|Hamasaka Onsen|ja|浜坂温泉|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hamasaka Onsen|1=Hamasaka Onsen|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11557617}} (9-minute bus ride) * {{ill|Yumura Onsen (Hyōgo)|ja|湯村温泉|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yumura Onsen (Hyōgo)|1=Q11563591|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11563591}} (15-minute bus ride) * {{ill|Hamasaka Prefectural Sun Beach|ja|浜坂県民サンビーチ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hamasaka Prefectural Sun Beach|1=Hamasaka Kenmin Sun Beach|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11557620}} * {{ill|Tajima Mihonoura|ja|但馬御火浦|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tajima Mihonoura|1=Tajima Mihonoura|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11381630}}

In {{ill|1868|en|1868|WD=Q7717|lt=1868|1=1868}} (the first year of the {{ill|Meiji era|en|Meiji era|WD=Q3125096|lt=Meiji era|1=Meiji era}}), it was renamed to its current title. In {{ill|1873|en|1873|WD=Q7757|lt=1873|1=1873}} (Meiji 6), it was designated a village shrine and enshrined {{ill|Hachiman|en|Hachiman|WD=Q261637|lt=Hachiman|1=Hachiman}} and the local {{ill|Placenta deity|ja|胞衣神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Placenta deity|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.
The shrine's main hall was rebuilt in {{ill|1444|en|1444|WD=Q6420|lt=1444|1=1444}} (Bun'an 1, {{ill|Muromachi period|en|Muromachi period|WD=Q334845|lt=Muromachi period|1=Muromachi period}}), but was destroyed in {{ill|1944|en|1944|WD=Q5268|lt=1944|1=1944}} (Shōwa 19) due to a typhoon. The current structure was rebuilt after World War II.
== Festivals and Events == * Annual Festival: September 29<ref>{{Cite web|title=Festival Calendar - Sangu Shrine|url=https://www.kirinnomachi-japan-heritage.jp/jp/reisai/1059/|access-date=2022-12-19|publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs}}</ref>
== Cultural Properties == * '''Shichikama {{ill|Kirin Shishi-mai|ja|麒麟獅子舞|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kirin Shishi-mai|ja_comment=fixed jawiki redirect|3=麒麟獅子|1=Qilin dance|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11677329}} Dance''' - Town-designated Intangible Folk Cultural Property (designated March 31, 1986)<ref>{{Cite web|title=Shichikama Kirin Shishi-mai|url=https://www.town.shinonsen.hyogo.jp/page/?mode=detail&page_id=8ab359199e3f3c495d46db6ceb3791ec|access-date=2022-12-19|publisher=Shin'onsen Town}}</ref> * '''Sangu Shrine Sacred Forest''' (山宮神社社叢) - Town-designated Natural Monument (designated March 31, 1986), featuring ancient {{ill|Castanopsis sieboldii|en|Castanopsis sieboldii|WD=Q846994|lt=Castanopsis|1=Castanopsis sieboldii}} and {{ill|Chamaecyparis obtusa|en|Chamaecyparis obtusa|WD=Q917187|lt=Hinoki cypress|1=Chamaecyparis obtusa}} trees, along with thick growths of {{ill|Camellia japonica|ja|ヤブツバキ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Camellia japonica|ja_comment=fixed jawiki redirect|3=ツバキ|1=Camellia japonica|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q160121}} and {{ill|Cleyera japonica|en|Cleyera japonica|WD=Q775470|lt=Sakaki|1=Cleyera japonica}}.<ref>{{Cite web|title=Sacred Forest of Shichikama Sangu Shrine|url=https://www.town.shinonsen.hyogo.jp/page/?mode=detail&page_id=b500773414376fa6bd4970ec294860e2|access-date=2022-12-19|publisher=Shin'onsen Town}}</ref>
== Access == * {{ill|West Japan Railway Company|en|West Japan Railway Company|WD=Q502125|lt=West Japan Railway Company|1=West Japan Railway Company}} (JR West) {{ill|San'in Main Line|en|San'in Main Line|WD=Q1135170|lt=San'in Main Line|1=San'in Main Line}}: From {{ill|Hamasaka Station|en|Hamasaka Station|WD=Q872032|lt=Hamasaka Station|1=Hamasaka Station}}, take the {{ill|Zentan Bus|ja|全但バス|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zentan Bus|1=Zentan Bus|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11389110}} bound for {{ill|Yumura Onsen (Hyōgo)|ja|湯村温泉|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yumura Onsen (Hyōgo)|1=Q11563591|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11563591}}, and get off at "Shichikama Onsen-guchi" bus stop. 5-minute walk. * From Osaka or Kobe, take the Zentan Bus {{ill|Yumechiyo-go|ja|夢千代号|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yumechiyo-go|ja_comment=fixed jawiki redirect|3=全但バス|1=Zentan Bus|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11389110}} to "Yumura Onsen," transfer to the bus for Hamasaka, and get off at "Shichikama Onsen-guchi." 5-minute walk.
== Nearby Attractions == * {{ill|Hamasaka Onsenkyō|ja|浜坂温泉郷|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hamasaka Onsenkyō|1=Hamasaka Onsenkyō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11557619}} ** {{ill|Hamasaka Onsen|ja|浜坂温泉|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hamasaka Onsen|1=Hamasaka Onsen|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11557617}} (9-minute bus ride) * {{ill|Yumura Onsen (Hyōgo)|ja|湯村温泉|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yumura Onsen (Hyōgo)|1=Q11563591|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11563591}} (15-minute bus ride) * {{ill|Hamasaka Prefectural Sun Beach|ja|浜坂県民サンビーチ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hamasaka Prefectural Sun Beach|1=Hamasaka Kenmin Sun Beach|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11557620}} * {{ill|Tajima Mihonoura|ja|但馬御火浦|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tajima Mihonoura|1=Tajima Mihonoura|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11381630}}
